# Storage Plug-ins for Containers

Hitachi Vantara's storage plug-ins for containers are **Kubernetes CSI extensions for Hitachi VSP storage**. They are not standalone storage systems — they are an integration layer that allows Kubernetes and OpenShift workloads to consume, replicate, and monitor the Hitachi VSP storage that your organisation already operates.

If you are evaluating Hitachi storage, start with the [VSP Storage](/vsp/index.md) documentation. These plug-ins are relevant once VSP storage is in place and you need to expose it to container workloads via the Container Storage Interface (CSI).

Three plug-ins are available, versioned and released as a suite at v3.17.4 (February–March 2026).

## Plugin Dependency Map

![Plugin dependency map: VSP Storage connects via REST API / FC / iSCSI to HSPC (Required Foundation), which provides CSI to Kubernetes. HSPC is required by HRPC (Replication Plug-in) and HSPP (Storage Plug-in for Prometheus). HRPC delivers Replication CRs to Kubernetes; HSPP pushes Metrics to Prometheus / Grafana.](/images/plugin-dependency-map.svg)
:::note
**HSPC is the required foundation.** HRPC and HSPP both require HSPC to be installed and configured first.
:::

## Plugin Overview

| Plugin                                                                            | Purpose                                       | Key Feature                                                   |
| --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [**HSPC** — Storage Plug-in for Containers](/container-plugins/hspc/index.md)     | CSI driver for Kubernetes persistent volumes  | Dynamic provisioning, snapshots, clones, volume expansion     |
| [**HRPC** — Replication Plug-in for Containers](/container-plugins/hrpc/index.md) | Automated storage replication across clusters | TrueCopy & Universal Replicator via Kubernetes CRs            |
| [**HSPP** — Storage Plug-in for Prometheus](/container-plugins/hspp/index.md)     | Prometheus exporter for VSP storage metrics   | 42 metrics covering capacity, IOPS, transfer rates, cache hit |

## Quick Start

### Install HSPC first
HSPC is the foundation for all three plugins. Start here regardless of which plugins you plan to use.
→ [Installing HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/installation.md)
### Add HRPC for replication (optional)
Install HRPC if you need automated storage replication between Kubernetes clusters for DR or data protection.
→ [Installing HRPC](/container-plugins/hrpc/v3.17.4/installation.md)
### Add HSPP for monitoring (optional)
Install HSPP to expose Hitachi VSP storage metrics to Prometheus and visualize them in Grafana.
→ [Installing HSPP](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/installation.md)

## Supported Environments

All three plugins share the same Kubernetes/OpenShift support matrix.

| Container Orchestrator               | v3.17.4 Support         |
| ------------------------------------ | ----------------------- |
| Kubernetes                           | 1.30 – 1.33             |
| Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform | 4.18 – 4.20             |
| Rancher Kubernetes Engine 2          | 1.30 – 1.33 (HSPC only) |
| Amazon EKS                           | 1.30 – 1.33 (HSPC only) |

→ [Full Compatibility Matrix](/container-plugins/compatibility.md)

## GitHub

All YAML manifests, sample files, and Operator definitions are available in the official repository:

[**github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi**](https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi)

## Related resources

- **[CSI Log Bundle (community)](/container-plugins/csi-log-bundle.md)** — optional scripts and browser viewer to package HSPC-related logs, cluster context, and (when applicable) DR-Operator data for troubleshooting. Upstream: [github.com/cmccuistion-hv/Hitachi-CSI-Log-Bundle](https://github.com/cmccuistion-hv/Hitachi-CSI-Log-Bundle).

| Red Hat OpenShift 4.20 | ✓ | ✓ | ✓ |
| Red Hat OpenShift 4.21 | ✓ | — | — |

## Storage Systems

Storage System Support| Software / Version | HSPCv3.18.0 | HRPCv3.17.4 | HSPPv3.17.4 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| VSP One Block High End (A0-05-20-00/05+) | FC, NVMe/TCP | — | FC |
| VSP One B24, B26, B28 (A3-04-20+) | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC |
| VSP E1090, E1090H (93-06-01+) | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC* | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC* |
| VSP 5100, 5500, 5100H, 5500H (90-04-04+) | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI |
| VSP 5200, 5600, 5200H, 5600H (90-08-01+) | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC* | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/FC* |
| VSP E590, E790, E990, E590H, E790H (93-02-04+) | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI |
| VSP G350, G370, G700, G900 (88-06-02+) | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI |
| VSP F350, F370, F700, F900 (88-06-02+) | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI | FC, iSCSI |
| VSP One SDS Block (01.08.00.0x+) | FC, iSCSI, NVMe/TCP | — | — |

*NVMe over FC requires specific minimum firmware versions. See individual Release Notes for exact version requirements.

## Monitoring Software (HSPP only)

| Software | Supported Version |
| --- | --- |
| Prometheus v2.x | v2.24.1 or later |
| Prometheus Pushgateway v1.x | v1.4.0 or later |

For the authoritative compatibility list, refer to the individual Release Notes or the [Product Compatibility Guide](https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com).




---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/index.md
---

# Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers (HSPC)

HSPC is a Kubernetes **Container Storage Interface (CSI) driver** for Hitachi VSP storage systems. It enables dynamic provisioning of Persistent Volumes, snapshots, clones, volume expansion, Stretched PVC (GAD), and resource partitioning directly from Kubernetes or OpenShift.

HSPC is also the **required foundation** for HRPC (replication) and HSPP (monitoring).

## Available Versions

| Version                                                | Status   | Release Date  | Doc Number       |
| ------------------------------------------------------ | -------- | ------------- | ---------------- |
| [**3.18.0**](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/index.md) | Latest   | March 2026    | MK-92ADPTR142-29 |
| [3.17.4](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/index.md)     | Archived | February 2026 | MK-92ADPTR142-28 |
| [3.16.1](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/index.md)     | Archived | 2025          | MK-92ADPTR142-22 |



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/index.md
---

- [Create VolumeSnapshotClass](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#create-volumesnapshotclass)
- [Create Static PV](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#create-static-pv)
- [Generate Support Bundle](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#generate-support-bundle)
- [Uninstall](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#uninstall)
- [Related](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#related)

- [Stretched PVC — Host Failure](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/troubleshooting.md#stretched-pvc--host-failure)

# Overview and Prerequisites

## Architecture

The following diagram shows how Storage Plug-in for Containers integrates a Kubernetes or OpenShift cluster with Hitachi storage via the Container Storage Interface (CSI):

![HSPC architecture — Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster: csi-controller as a Deployment on the control plane (REST API to Hitachi VSP storage), csi-node as a DaemonSet on each worker node (FC / iSCSI / NVMe data path to volumes)](/images/hspc/hspc-architecture.svg)

The `csi-controller` runs in the Control Plane and communicates with the storage system REST API. The `csi-node` pods run on each worker node and provide direct Fibre Channel / iSCSI / NVMe connectivity to storage volumes.

## Supported Container Orchestrators

| Container Orchestrator                  | Supported Versions | Notes                                                   |
| --------------------------------------- | ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform    | 4.19, 4.20, 4.21   | —                                                       |
| Kubernetes                              | 1.32, 1.33, 1.34   | —                                                       |
| Rancher Kubernetes Engine 2 (RKE2)      | 1.32, 1.33, 1.34   | References to "Kubernetes" in this guide apply to RKE2. |
| Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) | 1.32, 1.33, 1.34   | References to "Kubernetes" in this guide apply to EKS.  |

See the Product Compatibility Guide at [https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/hspc](https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/hspc) and [https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/sdsb-hspc](https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/sdsb-hspc) for supported products and versions.

## Server Requirements

| Component        | Requirement                                                                      |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| CPU              | x86\_64                                                                          |
| Operating system | See the [Release Notes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/release-notes.md)        |
| Interface        | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC, NVMe/TCP (bare metal); iSCSI, NVMe/TCP (VMs) |

## Storage Requirements

### VSP Family and VSP One Block

| Component              | Requirement                                                                                                    |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Model and SVOS version | See [Release Notes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/release-notes.md)                                          |
| Interface              | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC, NVMe/TCP (VSP One Block High End and VSP One Block 20 series only)         |
| Host group             | HSPC creates host groups using predefined naming rules. Do **not** use HSPC host groups for any other purpose. |
| User account           | Built-in Storage Administrator (View & Modify) user group (or custom group with equivalent roles)              |
| Required licenses      | Dynamic Provisioning (DP), Hitachi Thin Image (HTI), Hitachi Thin Image Advanced (HTIA) for VSP One Block      |
| SVP                    | Single and dual SVP supported. SVP is **not** required for VSP One Block.                                      |

### VSP One SDS Block

| Component    | Requirement                                                           |
| ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Version      | See [Release Notes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/release-notes.md) |
| Interface    | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe/TCP                                        |
| User account | Storage role (non-multitenancy); VpsStorage role (multitenancy)       |

## Network Requirements

HSPC uses **port 80 or 443** for REST API connections. Configure your firewall accordingly.

:::note
HSPC does **not** support IPv6. Use IPv4.
:::

## Pre-installation Tasks

:::tip
Before configuring your environment, review the [Hitachi CSI Best Practices Guide](/container-plugins/csi-best-practices/index.md) for recommendations on configuring and managing VSP storage systems and Hitachi CSI in Red Hat OpenShift and Kubernetes environments.
:::

### Server Pre-installation

| Component     | Task                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Hypervisor    | Set up the hypervisor if using virtual machines. Tested with VMware vSphere 7.0/8.0.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| Fibre Channel | Verify HBA is installed on nodes with FC connections.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| iSCSI         | Verify iSCSI initiator software is installed. If not installed, see [Creating an iSCSI initiator (RHEL 9)](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html/managing_storage_devices/configuring-an-iscsi-initiator_managing-storage-devices#creating-an-iscsi-initiator_configuring-an-iscsi-initiator). HSPC does **not** support IQNs with uppercase characters. |
| NVMe over FC  | Supported on Ubuntu 20.04/22.04/24.04, RHEL 9, and RHEL CoreOS. Install `nvme-cli`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| NVMe/TCP      | Supported on RHEL 9 and RHEL CoreOS. Install `nvme-cli` and load `nvme-tcp` module if needed.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Multipath     | Use **Device Mapper Multipath** for FC/iSCSI. Use **Native NVMe Multipath** for NVMe over FC/TCP.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |

:::note
Do **not** change a host NQN while HSPC is running. Drain the node, change the NQN, then restart HSPC. Each node must have a **unique NQN**.
:::

### Device Mapper Multipath Settings

Enable Device Mapper Multipath with `user_friendly_names yes`:

```text title="/etc/multipath.conf"
defaults {
  user_friendly_names yes
  find_multipaths yes
}
blacklist {
}
```

OS-specific documentation:

- [RHEL 8 DM Multipath](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/configuring_device_mapper_multipath/configuring-dm-multipath_configuring-device-mapper-multipath)
- [RHEL 9 DM Multipath](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html/configuring_device_mapper_multipath/configuring-dm-multipath_configuring-device-mapper-multipath)
- [Ubuntu Multipathing](https://ubuntu.com/server/docs/device-mapper-multipathing-introduction)

For **OpenShift**, apply multipath settings via MachineConfig:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
```

```bash
# Encode custom multipath settings
cat multipath-sample.conf | base64 -w0
```

```yaml title="multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
kind: MachineConfig
metadata:
  name: multipath-machineconfig-sample
  labels:
    machineconfiguration.openshift.io/role: worker
spec:
  config:
    ignition:
      version: 3.2.0
    storage:
      files:
        - contents:
            source: data:text/plain;charset=utf-8;base64,<base64-encoded-config>
          filesystem: root
          mode: 400
          path: /etc/multipath.conf
```

```bash
oc apply -f multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml
```

:::note
MachineConfig applies to compute nodes only. After creation, all compute nodes restart one by one, and `/etc/multipath.conf` is created on all compute nodes.
:::

### Storage Pre-installation for VSP Family and VSP One Block

| Component        | Task                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Program products | Enable DP, HTI, and HTIA (VSP One Block) licenses                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| Pool             | Create a DP pool. Dynamic Tiering is **not** supported. VSP One Block requires a pool configured with flash media (SSD).                                                                                                                                                            |
| FC connection    | Use a Fibre Channel switch. Configure ports: Connection Type=P-to-P, Fabric=ON, Security=Enabled.                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| iSCSI connection | Enable port security via Storage Navigator.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| NVMe over FC     | Create an NVM subsystem for the storage system. Enable namespace security and specify Linux as the host mode. Set the FC port to NVMe mode. **Disable LUN security on the Fibre Channel port.** Set an NVM subsystem port. The NVM subsystem must be dedicated exclusively to HSPC. |
| NVMe/TCP         | Create a dedicated NVM subsystem. Configure NVM subsystem ports. Supported on VSP One Block High End and VSP One Block 20 series.                                                                                                                                                   |

### Host Group and iSCSI Target Naming Rules \{#host-group-and-iscsi-target-naming-rules}

HSPC automatically creates and searches host groups using these naming rules.

:::tip
On **OpenShift**, the **Create Static PV** flow in the [CSI console plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#create-static-pv) enforces the `spc-` volume nickname pattern described below.
:::

**Host group naming rule:**

```
spc-<wwn1>-<wwn2>-<wwn3>
```

- `<wwn1>`, `<wwn2>`, `<wwn3>` are the sorted WWNs of the host.
- If a host has more than 3 WWNs, only the 3 lowest names are used.
- If a host has only one or two WWNs, the name is `spc-<wwn1>` or `spc-<wwn1>-<wwn2>` respectively.

:::note
As a best practice, do not create host groups manually. However, if you need to create one manually, ensure that you follow the naming rules above.
:::

:::note
Storage Plug-in for Containers will overwrite host mode options even if existing iSCSI targets have other host mode options.
:::

**iSCSI target naming rule:**

```
spc-<any-string>
```

- Auto-created as `spc-<hashed-IQN>`.
- Existing targets must be renamed to follow the `spc-` prefix rule.

## Multitenancy Settings (VSP One SDS Block) \{#multitenancy}

If you use the multitenancy functionality with VSP One SDS Block, configure the following:

:::note
In an environment where one Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster uses one VSP One SDS Block, the following restrictions apply to Virtual Private Storage (VPS):

- Multiple VPSs cannot be used.
- VPS and resources that do not belong to the VPS cannot be used at the same time.
  :::

| Component  | Task                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ---------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| VPS        | Create a VPS. Set the maximum number of compute nodes to a value greater than the number of nodes in the Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster. Set the maximum number of sessions to at least 20. |
| User group | Create a user group that belongs to the VPS. Set the scope to only the created VPS. Set the role to **VpsStorage**.                                                                       |
| User       | Create users and assign them to the user group you created. Do not assign users to any other user group.                                                                                  |



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/installation.md
---

# Installing Storage Plug-in for Containers

:::warning
If there is a previous version of HSPC installed, **remove it before proceeding** with installation.
:::


**OpenShift**

## Installation on OpenShift
HSPC is deployed on OpenShift using the Operator from OperatorHub.
### Access OperatorHub
Open the OpenShift web console and navigate to OperatorHub.
### Search and install the Operator
Search for **Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers** and install it.
:::note
Select the following settings in Operator Subscription:
- **Installation mode:** Select "A specific namespace on the cluster" and specify any namespace.
- **Update approval:** Select "Manual" and approve the Install Plan.
:::
For disconnected environments, mirror the `certified-operators` catalog before installation. See [OpenShift mirroring documentation](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/latest/disconnected/mirroring/installing-mirroring-installation-images.html). For example, for OpenShift Container Platform version 4.10, the index image of the certified-operators catalog is `registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.10`. For details, see [OLM RH Catalogs](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/latest/operators/understanding/olm-rh-catalogs.html).
### Confirm the Operator status is Succeeded
Verify the Operator Pod is **Running**.
### Create the HSPC instance
Click **Create Instance** on the Operator Details page. Click **Create**. For advanced configuration, see [Instance Configuration](#instance-configuration) below.
### Verify HSPC is ready
```bash
oc get hspc -n <namespace>
```
```
NAME   READY   AGE
hspc   true    30s
```
:::tip
Optional: install the [OpenShift CSI Console Plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md) (Technology Preview) for Hitachi-centric StorageClass views and guided tasks in the web console.
:::


**Kubernetes**

## Installation on Kubernetes
### Clone the repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/operator
```
### Create the Operator namespace
```bash
kubectl create -f hspc-operator-namespace.yaml
kubectl get namespaces | grep hspc-operator-system
```
```
hspc-operator-system   Active   53s
```
:::note
Verify that the namespace is used in all plugin configurations and operations.
:::
### Deploy the Operator
```bash
kubectl create -f hspc-operator.yaml
kubectl get deployment -n hspc-operator-system
```
```
NAME                                 READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
hspc-operator-controller-manager     1/1     1            1           14s
```
### Configure and create the HSPC instance
Edit `hspc_v1_hspc.yaml` if needed. For advanced configuration, see [Instance Configuration](#instance-configuration) below.
```bash
kubectl create -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
```
### Verify HSPC is ready
```bash
kubectl get hspc -n <namespace>
```
```
NAME   READY   AGE
hspc   true    30s
```


## Instance Configuration \{#instance-configuration}

Configure HSPC by editing the CustomResource YAML file (`hspc_v1_hspc.yaml`).

| Parameter                                    | Description                                                                                            |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `spec.imagePullSecrets`                      | Specify if a Secret is required to pull an image                                                       |
| `spec.controller.containers.name`            | Name of a container in `hspc-csi-controller` to configure (e.g., `hspc-csi-driver`, `csi-provisioner`) |
| `spec.controller.containers.image`           | Image name for `hspc-csi-controller`                                                                   |
| `spec.controller.containers.imagePullPolicy` | Image pull policy for `hspc-csi-controller` (default: `IfNotPresent`)                                  |
| `spec.controller.containers.env`             | Environment variables for `hspc-csi-controller`                                                        |
| `spec.controller.containers.args`            | Entry point arguments for `hspc-csi-controller`                                                        |
| `spec.controller.tolerations`                | Tolerations for the `hspc-csi-controller` Pod                                                          |
| `spec.controller.affinity.nodeAffinity`      | Node affinity for the `hspc-csi-controller` Pod                                                        |
| `spec.node.containers.name`                  | Name of a container in `hspc-csi-node` to configure                                                    |
| `spec.node.containers.image`                 | Image name for `hspc-csi-node`                                                                         |
| `spec.node.containers.env`                   | Environment variables for `hspc-csi-node`                                                              |
| `spec.node.tolerations`                      | Tolerations for the `hspc-csi-node` Pod                                                                |
| `spec.node.affinity.nodeAffinity`            | Node affinity for the `hspc-csi-node` Pod                                                              |

## Environment Variables

| Variable                 | Description                                                                 | Default |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| `SPC_VERIFY_CERTIFICATE` | If `true`, verifies the TLS certificate of the storage in HTTPS connections | `false` |
| `TZ`                     | Timezone for logging (e.g., `Asia/Tokyo`)                                   | `UTC`   |

### Example: Enabling Certificate Verification

```yaml title="hspc_v1_hspc.yaml"
apiVersion: csi.hitachi.com/v1
kind: HSPC
metadata:
  name: hspc
  namespace: <namespace>
spec:
  controller:
    containers:
      - name: hspc-csi-driver
        env:
          - name: SPC_VERIFY_CERTIFICATE
            value: "true"
```

After editing, uninstall and reinstall HSPC to apply the changes.

## Multipath Configuration via DaemonSet

Deploy and manage multipath settings on Kubernetes nodes through a DaemonSet:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/sample
kubectl create -f rosa-daemonset.yaml
```

Verify DaemonSet deployment:

```bash
kubectl get daemonset hspc-iscsi-init -n <namespace>
```

On each node, verify `multipathd` is active:

```bash
systemctl status multipathd
cat /etc/multipath.conf
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/configuration.md
---

# Configuring Storage Plug-in for Containers

:::note
Sample files are available at [github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi](https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi) in `csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/sample`.
:::

## Secret Settings \{#secret-settings}

The Secret contains the storage URL, username, password, and host mode options.

:::tip
On **OpenShift**, the HitachiCSI StorageClass view in the [console plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#hitachicsi-tab-on-storageclass-details) surfaces the secret name and namespace that correspond to these fields.
:::

```yaml title="secret-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample         # (1)
type: Opaque
data:
  url: aHR0cDovLzE3Mi4xNi4xLjE=   # (2) base64-encoded storage URL
  user: VXNlcjAx                    # (3) base64-encoded username
  password: UGFzc3dvcmQwMQ==        # (4) base64-encoded password
  hostModeOptions: ODgsODENCg==     # (5) base64-encoded host mode options
  resourceGroupID:                  # (6) Optional: resource group ID
  virtualStorageMachineSerialNumber: # (7) Optional: VSM serial number
```

| Field | Description                                                                                                                                                   |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | Secret name                                                                                                                                                   |
| (2)   | Base64-encoded storage URL. Use SVP IP for VSP 5000 series; controller IP for VSP E/F/G series; service IP for VSP One B20 series and VSP One Block High End. |
| (3)   | Base64-encoded username: `echo -n "User01" \| base64`                                                                                                         |
| (4)   | Base64-encoded password: `echo -n "Password01" \| base64`                                                                                                     |
| (5)   | Base64-encoded host mode options (e.g., `echo -n "88,81" \| base64`). Default options: 2, 22, 25, 68, 91.                                                     |
| (6)   | Optional: The resource group ID.                                                                                                                              |
| (7)   | Optional: The serial number of the virtual storage machine (VSM).                                                                                             |

:::note

- By default, the host mode options are set to 2, 22, 25, 68, and 91. If you need to add additional host mode options, specify them in the `hostModeOptions` parameter. You must delete and recreate the Pod to apply them.
- You cannot use VSM for the `resourceGroupID`.
  :::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/storage-classes.md
---

# Storage Classes and Advanced Usage

## StorageClass Settings \{#storageclass-settings}

The StorageClass file contains storage settings that are necessary for Storage Plug-in for Containers to work with your environment. The following samples provide information about the required parameters.

:::tip
On **OpenShift**, you can optionally use the [OpenShift CSI Console Plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md) (Technology Preview) to view Hitachi parameters on a StorageClass and start workflows such as clone, snapshot class, or static PV from the web console. The YAML below is the source of truth for the same fields.
:::

:::tip
For large-scale deployments, using **multiple StorageClasses with fewer ports each** (6 or fewer ports per StorageClass) significantly improves volume provisioning performance. See [Improving Volume Provisioning Performance at Scale](/container-plugins/csi-best-practices/openshift.md#provisioning-at-scale) in the CSI Best Practices Guide.
:::

:::warning
After creating a StorageClass and PVC, re-creating the StorageClass will **not** affect existing PVCs.
:::


**VSP Family / VSP One Block**

```yaml title="sc-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample                               # (1)
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"                         # (2)
  poolID: "1"                                   # (3)
  portID: CL1-A,CL2-A                           # (4)
  connectionType: fc                            # (5)
  storageEfficiency: "CompressionDeduplication" # (6)
  storageEfficiencyMode: "PostProcess"          # (7)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4               # (8)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"         # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"           # (10)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```
| Field | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | StorageClass name                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| (2)   | Storage serial number                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| (3)   | HDP pool ID                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| (4)   | Port ID (comma-separated for multipath). Not required for NVMe over FC or NVMe/TCP.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| (5)   | Connection type: `fc`, `iscsi`, `nvme-fc`, or `nvme-tcp`. Default: `fc`. For NVMe, also add `nvmSubsystemID`. NVMe/TCP is supported on VSP One Block High End and VSP One Block 20 series.                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| (6)   | Adaptive data reduction: `Compression`, `CompressionDeduplication`, or `Disabled`. Default: `Disabled`. For VSP One B20 series, `Disabled` is not supported — the default is `CompressionDeduplication`. If the LDEV was created with Storage Plug-in for Containers, do not change the parameters related to adaptive data reduction.                                                            |
| (7)   | Execution mode for adaptive data reduction: `Inline` or `PostProcess`. You can specify this parameter when (6) is `Compression` or `CompressionDeduplication`. If `storageEfficiencyMode` is not specified, adaptive data reduction runs in the default execution mode, which depends on the storage system model. See the Provisioning Guide for Open Systems or Provisioning Guide for details. |
| (8)   | Filesystem type: `ext4` or `xfs`. Default: `ext4`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| (9)   | Secret name                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| (10)  | Secret namespace                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                  |


**VSP One SDS Block**

```yaml title="sc-sample-vsp-one-sds-block.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample-vsp-one-sds-block    # (1)
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  storageType: vsp-one-sds-block       # (2) Required for VSP One SDS Block
  connectionType: fc                   # (3) fc, iscsi, or nvme-tcp
  storageEfficiency: Disabled          # (4) Compression or Disabled
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4      # (5)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```
| Field | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| (1)   | StorageClass name                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| (2)   | Storage type for SDS Block. Must be `vsp-one-sds-block`.                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| (3)   | Connection type: `fc`, `iscsi`, or `nvme-tcp`.                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| (4)   | Adaptive data reduction: `Compression` or `Disabled`. Default: `Disabled`. If you are using the multitenancy functionality, you cannot set this parameter — for multitenancy, the data reduction function settings comply with the VPS settings. |
| (5)   | Filesystem type: `ext4` or `xfs`. Default: `ext4`.                                                                                                                                                                                               |


## Static Provisioning \{#static-provisioning}

Use static provisioning to use an existing storage volume as a PersistentVolume.

:::tip
On **OpenShift**, the console plugin offers a **Create Static PV** wizard that aligns with the manifests in [Creating a PV](#creating-a-static-pv) and [Creating a PVC for Static Provisioning](#creating-a-static-pvc). See [OpenShift CSI Console Plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#console-ui).
:::

### Requirements

- The volume must exist on the storage system before creating the PV.
- The `claimRef` must be correctly specified to bind the PV to a PVC.

:::note
Static provisioning is not supported for stretched PVCs.
:::

:::note
If you are mixing dynamic and static provisioning, verify there are no PVs with `STATUS: Available` that have no `claimRef` set before creating new PVCs. Unbound PVs without `claimRef` may be incorrectly associated with dynamically-created PVCs.
:::

#### Requirements for VSP Family and VSP One Block

- The LDEV has the DP attribute. For VSP family, the LDEV does not have the DRS attribute. For VSP One Block, the LDEV has the DRS attribute. (The DRS attribute indicates the data reduction shared volume.)
- The nickname format is `spc-<10-digit-hexadecimal-number>`. The hex value must be unique for each LDEV. You can generate a unique string with: `echo spc-$(cat /dev/urandom | tr -dc a-f0-9 | head -c 10)`
- The LDEV is not mapped to a port.
- No pairs are formed.

If the LDEV is assigned to a specific resource group, also see [Resource Partitioning](#resource-partitioning) for storage system requirements.

#### Requirements for VSP One SDS Block

- `naaId` can be obtained as volume information. Volumes created for VSP One SDS Block v1.11 or earlier do not support `naaId` and are not supported.
- `volumeType` is `Normal`.
- `name` and `nickname` have the same value, in the format `spc-<10-digit-hexadecimal-number>`. You can generate a unique string with: `echo spc-$(cat /dev/urandom | tr -dc a-f0-9 | head -c 10)`
- The volume is not connected to a computer node.

If the volume is assigned to a specific VPS, also see the multitenancy configuration in [Overview and Prerequisites](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/overview.md#multitenancy).

### Creating Secret and StorageClass for Static Provisioning

Use the same Secret and StorageClass format as for dynamic provisioning. See [Configuration](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/configuration.md).

### Creating a PV \{#creating-a-static-pv}

Specify the LDEV ID and other volume properties in the PV manifest.

```yaml title="pv-static-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: pv-static-sample
  annotations:
    pv.kubernetes.io/provisioned-by: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
spec:
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  capacity:
    storage: 10Gi
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy: Retain
  volumeMode: Filesystem
  csi:
    driver: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
    volumeHandle: "<serial-number>--<connection-type>--<storage-device-id>--<ldev-id-dec>--<nickname>"
    fsType: ext4
    nodeStageSecretRef:
      name: secret-sample
      namespace: default
    nodePublishSecretRef:
      name: secret-sample
      namespace: default
    controllerPublishSecretRef:
      name: secret-sample
      namespace: default
    volumeAttributes:
      connectionType: fc
      portID: CL1-A,CL2-A
      serialNumber: "54321"
  claimRef:
    name: pvc-static-sample
    namespace: default
```

### Creating a PVC for Static Provisioning \{#creating-a-static-pvc}

Bind a PVC to the static PV by specifying `volumeName`:

```yaml title="pvc-static-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-static-sample
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  volumeName: pv-static-sample
```

## Snapshots \{#snapshots}

:::note
If the volume has been expanded, verify that the operation has successfully completed before creating the volume snapshot. See [Expanding Volumes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/expanding-volumes.md) for details. Flush the data before creating a snapshot for data consistency. For example, temporarily remove the Pod. This feature is not supported on VSP One SDS Block.
:::

:::note
If your environment is Kubernetes, install Snapshot CRDs and Snapshot Controller per cluster. See [https://github.com/kubernetes-csi/external-snapshotter](https://github.com/kubernetes-csi/external-snapshotter). For Snapshot CRDs, use v1. If Snapshot Alpha or Beta CRDs are present in your environment, remove them before installing Snapshot v1 CRDs.
:::

Create a VolumeSnapshotClass before using snapshots:

:::tip
On **OpenShift**, you can create a `VolumeSnapshotClass` from the HitachiCSI view on a StorageClass. See [OpenShift CSI Console Plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#create-volumesnapshotclass) and compare with the manifest below.
:::

```yaml title="volumesnapshotclass-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshotClass
metadata:
  name: snapshotclass-sample
driver: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
deletionPolicy: Delete
parameters:
  poolID: "1"
  retentionPeriod: "1"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-namespace: "default"
```

| Field                                             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `poolID`                                          | Same `poolID` as the StorageClass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `retentionPeriod`                                 | Optional. Specify a value between 1 and 12,288 hours to create immutable snapshots. Once configured, the snapshot data remains protected from updates until the retention period expires — it cannot be deleted or cloned during this time. After the period ends, the retention setting is automatically disabled. Immutable snapshots are supported only on VSP One B20 series storage systems. |
| `csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-name`      | Same Secret name as the StorageClass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-namespace` | Same Secret namespace as the StorageClass.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |

Create a snapshot of a PVC:

```yaml title="snapshot-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshot
metadata:
  name: snapshot-sample
spec:
  volumeSnapshotClassName: snapshotclass-sample
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: pvc-sample
```

Restore a PVC from a snapshot:

```yaml title="pvc-from-snapshot.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-from-snapshot
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  dataSource:
    name: snapshot-sample
    kind: VolumeSnapshot
    apiGroup: snapshot.storage.k8s.io
```

## Cloning a PVC \{#cloning-a-pvc}

:::note
If the volume is expanded, confirm that expansion has completed before executing this feature. See [Expanding Volumes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/expanding-volumes.md) for details. Flush the data before cloning for data consistency. For example, temporarily remove the Pod. This feature is not supported on VSP One SDS Block.
:::

Clone an existing PVC:

:::note
The OpenShift console plugin’s **Clone StorageClass** action duplicates a **StorageClass**, not a PVC. For StorageClass-oriented UI, see [OpenShift CSI Console Plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#clone-storageclass). PVC cloning in the console follows the same `dataSource` pattern as this YAML.
:::

```yaml title="pvc-clone-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-clone-sample
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  dataSource:
    name: pvc-sample
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
```

## Resource Partitioning

Resource partitioning enables you to allocate and isolate storage system resources for each Kubernetes cluster using Hitachi Virtual Storage Machine (VSM) resource groups. HSPC supports the use of multiple resource groups, multiple VSMs, and meta-resource groups within a single cluster. Each resource group is identified by a unique `resourceGroupID`, which is specified in the Secret.

:::note
Resource partitioning is not supported for VSP One SDS Block. If you use resource partitioning, configure it **before** creating any StorageClasses or PVCs.
:::

**Example: multiple clusters sharing resource groups across storage systems:**

![Resource partitioning multi-cluster overview — four Kubernetes clusters mapped across two resource groups: production #1 and #2 use Resource Group #1 (blue), test #1 and #2 use Resource Group #2 (pink), accessed via Shared Ports](/images/hspc/resource-partitioning-overview.svg)

Storage ports are shared but LDEV IDs, Host Group IDs, and Pools are isolated per resource group.

**Unsupported (Example 1) — mixing a resource group StorageClass with meta\_resource in the same cluster:**

![Resource partitioning unsupported example 1 — single Kubernetes cluster with both a resource-group-specific StorageClass and a meta\_resource StorageClass](/images/hspc/resource-partitioning-unsupported.svg)

You cannot use both a resource-group-specific Secret/StorageClass and a meta\_resource (no resource group) Secret/StorageClass in the same Kubernetes cluster.

**Unsupported (Example 2) — two Resource Groups from the same storage mapped to one cluster:**

![Resource partitioning unsupported example 2 — single Kubernetes cluster with StorageClasses for both Resource Group #1 and Resource Group #2 from the same storage system](/images/hspc/resource-partitioning-unsupported2.svg)

You cannot map multiple resource groups from the same storage system to the same Kubernetes cluster.

### Secret Settings for Resource Partitioning

Specify the resource group ID and optionally the virtual storage machine (VSM) serial number in the Secret:

```yaml title="secret-resource-partitioned.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample
type: Opaque
data:
  url: <base64-encoded-url>
  user: <base64-encoded-user>
  password: <base64-encoded-password>
  hostModeOptions: <base64-encoded-options>    # (5) Optional
  resourceGroupID: <base64-encoded-rg-id>      # (6) Optional
  virtualStorageMachineSerialNumber: <base64-encoded-vsm-serial>  # (7) Optional
```

| Field                               | Description                                                           |
| ----------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `resourceGroupID`                   | (6) Optional. The resource group ID of the storage system.            |
| `virtualStorageMachineSerialNumber` | (7) Optional. The serial number of the virtual storage machine (VSM). |

:::note
You cannot use VSM for the `resourceGroupID`.
:::

### StorageClass Settings for Resource Partitioning

Add the `resourceGroup` parameter to the StorageClass:

```yaml title="sc-resource-partitioned.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-resource-partitioned
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"
  poolID: "1"
  portID: CL1-A,CL2-A
  connectionType: fc
  resourceGroup: "1"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```

:::warning
All replications on a storage system using resource partitioning with HRPC must belong to the same resource group. See [HRPC Appendix](/container-plugins/hrpc/v3.17.4/appendix.md#resource-partitioning) for HRPC-specific requirements.
:::

## Stretched PVC

Stretched PVC provides high-availability by spanning a PVC across two storage systems at two different sites. It uses **GAD (Global Active Device)** — Hitachi's active-active synchronous replication technology — so both storage systems serve I/O simultaneously and data is always consistent across sites.

The following diagram shows the topology of a Stretched Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster across two sites with GAD replication:

![Stretched PVC topology — Site A and Site B nodes in a single stretched Kubernetes cluster, each connected to their own Storage System with GAD (Global Active Device) active-active replication in between](/images/hspc/stretched-pvc-topology.svg)

The following diagram shows the data path connections used by Stretched PVC (straight path and cross path):

![Stretched PVC data paths — straight path from each node to its local storage system and cross path from each node to the remote storage system](/images/hspc/stretched-pvc-paths.svg)

### Requirements

:::note
Stretched PVC supports only **Fibre Channel** and **iSCSI** connections.
:::

**Cluster requirements:**

| Item                          | Requirement                                                                                                                  |
| ----------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cluster configuration         | A single Kubernetes or OpenShift cluster (stretched cluster) spanning two sites, with storage systems allocated at each site |
| REST API connection           | HSPC must be able to communicate with the storage systems at both the primary and secondary sites via the REST API           |
| Data path connection          | Each node must be able to perform data communication with the storage systems at both the primary and secondary sites        |
| Resource group / VSM settings | Each node must be able to access one or more resource groups or VSMs from the storage systems at both sites                  |

**Storage system requirements (both sites):**

| Item                     | Requirement                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Supported models         | VSP One Block High End, VSP One Block 20 series, VSP E series, VSP 5000 series, VSP F350/F370/F700/F900, VSP G350/G370/G700/G900                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| License                  | Global Active Device (GAD) license — see the Global-Active Device User Guide                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| Virtual storage machines | For each storage system, create one or more VSMs. Primary and secondary VSMs must have the same serial number and model. Use VSMs exclusively for HSPC. When using the same combination of storage systems from multiple clusters: use the same VSMs for each cluster, and ensure the primary-site/secondary-site pairing is consistent across clusters. |
| Users and user groups    | Create a user group with **Storage Administrator (View & Modify)** and **Security Administrator** privileges, and add users to it.                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| Remote paths             | Create remote paths. The same path group ID must be used for both the primary site and the secondary site.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| Quorum disks             | Create quorum disks. The same quorum disk ID must be used for both the primary site and the secondary site.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |

**Compatibility with other features:**

| Feature                 | Compatible with Stretched PVC?                                                                                                        |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Volume snapshots        | Yes — see [Snapshots for a Stretched PVC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/snapshots.md#snapshots-for-a-stretched-pvc) |
| Volume cloning          | Yes — see [Cloning from a Stretched PVC](#cloning-from-stretched-pvc)                                                                 |
| Volume expansion        | Yes                                                                                                                                   |
| Raw block volume        | Yes                                                                                                                                   |
| ReadWriteMany           | Yes                                                                                                                                   |
| ReadOnlyMany            | Yes                                                                                                                                   |
| Adaptive data reduction | Yes                                                                                                                                   |
| Static provisioning     | No                                                                                                                                    |

**Using a standard PVC in a stretched cluster:**

To use a regular (non-stretched) PVC in a stretched cluster, configure resource groups in advance (see [Resource Partitioning](#resource-partitioning)).

:::note
For host groups, use ports that are **not** used by Stretched PVCs. To control which site a Pod is deployed to, use the **Node Affinity** or **Node Selector** features of Kubernetes/OpenShift.
:::

### Creating a Stretched PVC

When HSPC receives the Secret and StorageClass for a Stretched PVC, it creates volumes at both the primary and secondary sites and generates a global-active device pair. Use Storage Navigator to manage the status of the global-active device pair between creation and deletion of the Stretched PVC.

#### Create the Secret
```yaml title="secret-sample-stretched.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample-stretched
  namespace: hvplugin
type: Opaque
stringData:
  primarySerial: "11111"              # (1) Storage system serial number at the primary site
  primaryURL: https://172.16.0.1      # (2) Storage system REST API URL at the primary site
  primaryUser: primary-user1          # (3) Storage system user at the primary site
  primaryPassword: primary-password1  # (4) Password of the storage system user at the primary site
  secondarySerial: "22222"            # (5) Storage system serial number at the secondary site
  secondaryURL: https://172.16.0.2    # (6) Storage system REST API URL at the secondary site
  secondaryUser: secondary-user1      # (7) Storage system user at the secondary site
  secondaryPassword: secondary-password1 # (8) Password of the storage system user at the secondary site
  virtualStorageSerialNumber: "3333"  # (9) Virtual storage system serial number
```
:::note
The virtual serial number must be the same at both the primary and secondary sites. If you want to use separate VSMs, create a separate Secret for each VSM.
:::
:::warning
After creating a Stretched PVC using a secret that specifies primary and secondary, you **cannot** interchange the primary and secondary values in that secret.
:::
#### Create the StorageClass
```yaml title="sc-sample-stretched.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample-stretched
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  connectionType: fc
  replicationType: stretched              # (1)
  copyGroupName: "spc-copy-group-name"    # (2) Optional
  copyPairName: "spc-copy-pair-name"      # (3) Optional
  consistencyGroupId: "1"                 # (4) Optional
  quorumID: "30"                          # (5)
  primaryPoolID: "10"                     # (6)
  primaryPortID: CL1-A,CL2-A             # (7)
  secondaryPoolID: "20"                   # (8)
  secondaryPortID: CL1-F                  # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```
| #   | Parameter                    | Description                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| --- | ---------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| (1) | `replicationType: stretched` | Identifies this as a Stretched PVC StorageClass                                                                                                                                                 |
| (2) | `copyGroupName`              | Optional. Unique, case-sensitive, 1–29 characters. **Must start with `spc-` prefix** — omitting the prefix will cause an error.                                                                 |
| (3) | `copyPairName`               | Optional. Unique, case-sensitive, 1–31 characters. **Must start with `spc-` prefix** — omitting the prefix will cause an error. The `copyPairName` name is unique to the copy group.            |
| (4) | `consistencyGroupId`         | Optional. Consistency group ID (decimal number). `copyPairName` and `consistencyGroupId` are applied only when a custom `copyGroupName` is provided — otherwise the system uses default values. |
| (5) | `quorumID`                   | ID of the quorum disk                                                                                                                                                                           |
| (6) | `primaryPoolID`              | Storage system pool ID at the primary site                                                                                                                                                      |
| (7) | `primaryPortID`              | Storage system port ID at the primary site. Use commas to delimit multiple ports for multipath.                                                                                                 |
| (8) | `secondaryPoolID`            | Storage system pool ID at the secondary site                                                                                                                                                    |
| (9) | `secondaryPortID`            | Storage system port ID at the secondary site                                                                                                                                                    |
#### Create a PVC and verify it is Bound
Create a PVC that references the StorageClass from the previous step, then verify:
```bash
kubectl get pvc <pvc-name>
```
The PVC `STATUS` must be `Bound`.
:::note
Creating a Stretched PVC takes more time than creating a regular PVC.
:::

### GAD Pair Split and Resync

Use the `GadPairAction` custom resource to split or resync a GAD pair. This is useful for maintenance, failover testing, or disaster recovery scenarios.

```yaml title="gadpairaction-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: csi.hitachi.com/v1
kind: GadPairAction
metadata:
  name: gadpairaction-sample
  namespace: <namespace>
spec:
  action: split                          # (1)
  remoteMirrorCopyPairId: <copy-pair-id> # (2) Optional
  storageClassName: sc-stretched         # (3)
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: pvc-stretched-sample  # (4) Optional
  retryPolicy:
    maxRetries: 2                        # (5) Optional
    interval: 120s                       # (6) Optional
```

Apply the action and monitor its status:

```bash
kubectl apply -f gadpairaction-sample.yaml
kubectl describe gadpairaction gadpairaction-sample -n <namespace>
```

| Field                                   | Description                                                                                                            |
| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `spec.action`                           | `split` to suspend replication, or `resync` to resume replication                                                      |
| `spec.remoteMirrorCopyPairId`           | Optional. The copy pair ID. If not specified, the pair is identified using the PVC name.                               |
| `spec.storageClassName`                 | The Stretched PVC StorageClass name                                                                                    |
| `spec.source.persistentVolumeClaimName` | Optional. Scope the action to a specific PVC. If not specified, the pair is identified using `remoteMirrorCopyPairId`. |
| `spec.retryPolicy.maxRetries`           | Optional. Maximum number of retry attempts (integer, e.g. `2`).                                                        |
| `spec.retryPolicy.interval`             | Optional. Time interval between retry attempts (e.g. `120s` or `2mins`).                                               |

:::note
Stretched PVC metrics collection by HSPP is **not supported**.
:::

### Stretched PVC with Adaptive Data Reduction (ADR)

HSPC supports Adaptive Data Reduction (ADR) for Stretched PVCs provisioned using global-active device volumes. Add `storageEfficiency` and `storageEfficiencyMode` parameters to the StorageClass:

#### Create the StorageClass with ADR parameters
```yaml title="sc-stretched-adr.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample-stretched-adr
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  connectionType: fc
  quorumID: "4"
  primaryPoolID: "1"
  primaryPortID: CL1-A
  secondaryPoolID: "2"
  secondaryPortID: CL1-B
  storageEfficiency: "CompressionDeduplication"    # (1)
  storageEfficiencyMode: "PostProcess"              # (2)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```
| Parameter               | Description                                     |
| ----------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `storageEfficiency`     | `"CompressionDeduplication"` or `"Compression"` |
| `storageEfficiencyMode` | `"PostProcess"` or `"Inline"`                   |
#### Create a PVC and verify it is Bound
Create a PVC by specifying the StorageClass created in the previous step, then verify:
```bash
kubectl get pvc <pvc-name>
```
The PVC `STATUS` must be `Bound`.
:::note
Creating a Stretched PVC takes more time than creating a regular PVC.
:::

### Cloning from a Stretched PVC \{#cloning-from-stretched-pvc}

You can replicate Stretched PVC data to a regular PVC by creating a snapshot from the Stretched PVC and then cloning a new regular PVC from that snapshot.

**Before you begin**, configure the storage system for use with a regular PVC:

| Item                | Requirement                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Storage system      | Specify either the primary site or the secondary site                                                                                                                                                  |
| Storage system user | Assign both the resource group for the virtual storage machine used for the Stretched PVC and the resource group for the regular PVC. Do not assign other resource groups.                             |
| LDEV                | Assign the required LDEV IDs to the resource group for the regular PVC. The assigned IDs must use **sequential numbers** — if the numbers are not sequential, the operations might not work correctly. |

#### Create the Stretched PVC
See [Creating a Stretched PVC](#creating-a-stretched-pvc).
#### Create a snapshot of the Stretched PVC
See [Snapshots for a Stretched PVC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/snapshots.md#snapshots-for-a-stretched-pvc).
#### Create a Secret for the regular PVC
See [Configuration](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/configuration.md).
#### Create a StorageClass for the regular PVC
See [StorageClass Settings](#storageclass-settings).
#### Create a PVC from the Stretched PVC snapshot
```yaml title="pvc-from-stretched-snapshot-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-from-stretched-snapshot-sample
spec:
  dataSource:
    name: VolumeSnapshot-stretched
    kind: VolumeSnapshot
    apiGroup: snapshot.storage.k8s.io
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
```
```bash
kubectl apply -f pvc-from-stretched-snapshot-sample.yaml
```

### Monitoring Stretched PVC Usage in Pods

Similar to a regular PVC, you can use a Stretched PVC from a Pod. When you create a Pod, HSPC outputs a log message showing the serial numbers of the storage systems that successfully connected to the Pod:

```
[INFO]storages used for publish : [11111 22222]
```

Typically, both the primary and secondary site storage systems are used. However, depending on system status, only one storage system (either primary or secondary) may be connected.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/provisioning.md
---

# Provisioning Volumes

## PersistentVolumeClaim Settings

```yaml title="pvc-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample        # (1)
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce        # (2)
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi         # (3)
  storageClassName: sc-sample  # (4)
```

| Field | Description                                         |
| ----- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | PVC name                                            |
| (2)   | `ReadWriteOnce`, `ReadWriteMany`, or `ReadOnlyMany` |
| (3)   | Volume size                                         |
| (4)   | StorageClass name                                   |

:::note

- For `ReadWriteMany`, see [Raw Block Volume](#raw-block-volume) below.
- For `ReadOnlyMany`, see [ReadOnlyMany](#readonlymany) below.
- To use an existing storage volume, see [Static Provisioning](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/storage-classes.md#static-provisioning).
- On **OpenShift**, optional static PV and PVC creation from the web console is described in [OpenShift CSI Console Plugin](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.18.0/quick-reference/csi-console-plugin.md#create-static-pv).

:::

## Pod Settings

```yaml title="pod-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: pod-sample           # (1)
spec:
  containers:
    - name: my-busybox
      image: busybox
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: "/data"  # (2)
          name: sample-volume
      command: ["sleep", "1000000"]
      imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  volumes:
    - name: sample-volume
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: pvc-sample # (3)
```

## Full Workflow — Create and Verify

```bash title="Create all resources"
kubectl create -f secret-sample.yaml
kubectl create -f sc-sample.yaml
kubectl create -f pvc-sample.yaml
kubectl create -f pod-sample.yaml
```

Verify each resource:

```bash
kubectl get secret
kubectl get sc
kubectl get pvc
kubectl get pod
```

Expected output:

```
NAME         STATUS   VOLUME                                     CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   STORAGECLASS   AGE
pvc-sample   Bound    pvc-cf8c6089-0386-4c39-8037-e1520a986a7d  1Gi        RWO            sc-sample      28s
```

```bash
kubectl describe pv <pv-name>
```

```
VolumeHandle: 01--scsi--900000070010--914--spc-ecdf100f22
VolumeAttributes:
  connectionType=fc
  ldevIDDec=914
  ldevIDHex=03:92
  nickname=spc-ecdf100f22
  ports=CL5-C
  size=1Gi
```

:::warning
Do **not** change the nickname of an LDEV created by HSPC.
:::

## Delete Resources
