# Installing Storage Plug-in for Prometheus

HSPP and Prometheus Pushgateway are installed as Kubernetes Deployments.

## Before You Begin

- Install Kubernetes or Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform.
- Install [HSPC (Storage Plug-in for Containers)](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/installation.md).
- Configure `StorageClass` for HSPC.

## Procedure

### Clone the repository and navigate to the HSPP version directory
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspp/<hspp_version>
```
The YAML files are stored in `csi-operator-hitachi/hspp/<hspp_version>/yaml`.
### Configure the storage secret
Modify `yaml/secret-sample.yaml` with your storage system credentials. See [Configuring HSPP via YAML file](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/configuration.md#yaml-configuration-file) for parameter details.
```yaml title="yaml/secret-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: storage-exporter-secret
  namespace: hspc-monitoring-system
type: Opaque
stringData:
  storage-exporter.yaml: |-
    storages:
      - serial: 54321
        url: http://172.16.1.1
        user: MaintenanceUser
        password: Password
```
### Create the namespace
```bash
kubectl apply -f yaml/namespace.yaml
```
### Install HSPP and Prometheus Pushgateway
```bash
kubectl apply -f yaml/secret-sample.yaml -f yaml/exporter.yaml
```

After installation, proceed to [Deploying Prometheus and Grafana](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/deploying-prometheus-grafana.md).



---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/deploying-prometheus-grafana.md
---

# Deploying and Configuring Prometheus and Grafana

After installing HSPP and Prometheus Pushgateway, deploy and configure Prometheus and Grafana.

- **Prometheus** — collects metrics from HSPP via Prometheus Pushgateway
- **Grafana** — dashboard software that visualizes the metrics


**Production environment**

## Deploying to Production
For production environments, download and deploy Prometheus and Grafana independently. See [prometheus.io](https://prometheus.io/) and [grafana.com](https://grafana.com/) for full documentation.
### Connect Prometheus to the Pushgateway
In your `prometheus.yml` file, add the Pushgateway scrape job under `scrape_configs`:
```yaml title="prometheus.yml"
scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'hspc_monitoring_job'
    scrape_interval: 1m
    honor_labels: true
    static_configs:
      - targets:
          - pushgateway.hspc-monitoring-system.svc.cluster.local:9091
```
### Import the sample Grafana dashboard
Import `grafana/sample.json` into Grafana.
:::note
The sample dashboard was exported from Grafana 7 and may not be compatible with other Grafana versions.
:::


**Test environment**

## Deploying to a Test Environment
:::warning
This procedure is for **testing or demo purposes only**. For production deployments, see the Production tab.
:::
### Update the StorageClass name in the sample manifest
```bash
sed -i.bak \
  -e "s/sc-sample/<StorageClass-name>/g" \
  yaml/grafana-prometheus-sample.yaml
```
### (Optional) Modify the Grafana service
The `grafana-prometheus-sample.yaml` file exposes Grafana as a `NodePort` with a random port. Modify the file if you want a different service type.
### (OpenShift only) Create security context constraints
```bash
oc apply -f yaml/scc-for-openshift.yaml
```
### Deploy Grafana and Prometheus
```bash
kubectl apply -f yaml/grafana-prometheus-sample.yaml
```
### Access Grafana
```bash
kubectl get svc -n hspc-monitoring-system
```
Access Grafana at `<Node-IP>:<Grafana-Port>`.
Default credentials: **admin / secret**


## Setting Up Logging

Redirect HSPP logs to files or a logging backend for debugging and monitoring.

```bash
kubectl logs -n hspc-monitoring-system <storage-exporter-pod-name>
```

For cluster-level logging setup, see [Kubernetes logging documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/logging/).



---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/configuration.md
---

# Configuring Storage Plug-in for Prometheus

HSPP (Deployment name: `storage-exporter`) can be configured two ways: environment variables or the YAML configuration file.

## Environment Variables \{#environment-variables}

| Environment Variable             | Default Value                                        | Description                                                                                                                                                                         |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `SPC_PUSHGATEWAY_URL`            | `http://pushgateway:9091`                            | Prometheus Pushgateway URL                                                                                                                                                          |
| `SPC_JOB_NAME`                   | `hspc_monitoring_job`                                | Prometheus job name. See [Prometheus jobs and instances](https://prometheus.io/docs/concepts/jobs_instances/).                                                                      |
| `SPC_CONF_PATH`                  | `/spc/storage-exporter/config/storage-exporter.yaml` | Configuration file path                                                                                                                                                             |
| `SPC_VERIFY_CERTIFICATE`         | `false`                                              | If `true`, HSPP verifies TLS certificates for storage connections                                                                                                                   |
| `SPC_ENABLE_DEBUG_LOG`           | `false`                                              | If `true`, enables debug logging. Set to `false` if logs become too large.                                                                                                          |
| `SPC_INTERVAL`                   | `300`                                                | Data collection interval in seconds. Valid range: 300–600. See [Troubleshooting data collection issues](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/troubleshooting.md#data-collection-issues). |
| `SPC_DISABLE_METRIC_NAME_PREFIX` | `false`                                              | If `true`, removes the `spc_` prefix from metric names. For backwards compatibility with versions 1.1.0 and earlier. See [Upgrading](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/upgrading.md). |
| `TZ`                             | `UTC`                                                | Timezone for logging. For example, `Asia/Tokyo`.                                                                                                                                    |

## YAML Configuration File \{#yaml-configuration-file}

Configure HSPP via `yaml/secret-sample.yaml`, which is mounted as a Kubernetes Secret at the path defined by `SPC_CONF_PATH`.

### Parameters

| Parameter           | Description                                                                                                                            |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `storages`          | List of storage system configurations                                                                                                  |
| `storages.serial`   | Storage system serial number                                                                                                           |
| `storages.url`      | Storage system API URL. Use SVP IP for VSP 5000 series; storage controller IP for VSP E/F/G series; service IP for VSP One B20 series. |
| `storages.user`     | Username for the storage system. Must belong to the **Administrator** user group.                                                      |
| `storages.password` | Password for the user                                                                                                                  |

:::note
All storage system resources must be made available to HSPP. See the System Administrator Guide for details.
:::

### Example Configuration

```yaml title="yaml/secret-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: storage-exporter-secret
  namespace: hspc-monitoring-system
type: Opaque
stringData:
  storage-exporter.yaml: |-
    storages:
      - serial: 54321
        url: http://172.16.1.1
        user: MaintenanceUser
        password: Password
```

:::tip
To monitor multiple storage systems, add additional entries under `storages`:

```yaml
storages:
  - serial: 54321
    url: http://172.16.1.1
    user: MaintenanceUser
    password: Password
  - serial: 67890
    url: http://172.16.1.2
    user: AdminUser
    password: Password2
```

:::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/upgrading.md
---

# Upgrading Storage Plug-in for Prometheus

## Before You Begin

Upgrade [Storage Plug-in for Containers (HSPC)](/container-plugins/hspc/index.md) first.

:::note Metric name prefix change
Starting in **version 1.2.0**, the prefix `spc_` is attached to all metric names by default.

If you are upgrading from version 1.1.0 or earlier and want to preserve the old metric names (without the `spc_` prefix), set the `SPC_DISABLE_METRIC_NAME_PREFIX` environment variable of the `storage-exporter` Deployment to `true`.

See [Environment Variables](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/configuration.md#environment-variables) for details.
:::

## Procedure

### Clone the repository and navigate to the target version
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspp/<hspp_version>
```
### Apply the updated exporter manifest
```bash
kubectl apply -f yaml/exporter.yaml
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/uninstalling.md
---

# Uninstalling Storage Plug-in for Prometheus

Uninstalling HSPP deletes the `storage-exporter` Deployment and Prometheus Pushgateway.

:::note
Collected metric data in Prometheus is **not deleted** if Prometheus is deployed in a namespace different from `hspc-monitoring-system`.
:::

```bash
kubectl delete -f yaml/
```

This deletes all HSPP resources including the `storage-exporter` Deployment, Pushgateway, and the `hspc-monitoring-system` namespace resources.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/metrics.md
---

# Prometheus Metric Reference

Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Prometheus collects the following metrics from Kubernetes and Hitachi VSP storage system resources. Metrics are collected at configurable intervals (default: every **5 minutes**).

:::note
By setting the [`SPC_DISABLE_METRIC_NAME_PREFIX`](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/configuration.md#environment-variables) environment variable to `true`, you can remove the `spc_` prefix from all metric names (for backwards compatibility with versions 1.1.0 and earlier).
:::

All typesgaugeShowing 42 of 42 metrics| Metric Name | Type | Description | Labels |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| spc_volume_total_capacity | gauge | Total volume capacity. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_used_capacity | gauge | Used volume capacity. Note: there is a difference between capacity reported by the storage system and by the OS. Verify capacity from the OS as well. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_free_capacity | gauge | Free volume capacity. Note: there is a difference between capacity from the storage system and the OS. Verify capacity from the OS as well. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_used_capacity_rate | gauge | Used volume capacity rate. Note: there is a difference between capacity from the storage system and the OS. Verify capacity from the OS as well. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_total_read_iops | gauge | Sum of random and sequential read IOPS for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_random_read_iops | gauge | Random read IOPS for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_sequential_read_iops | gauge | Sequential read IOPS for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_total_write_iops | gauge | Sum of random and sequential write IOPS for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_random_write_iops | gauge | Random write IOPS for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_sequential_write_iops | gauge | Sequential write IOPS for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_average_write_response_time | gauge | Average write response time (microseconds). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_average_read_response_time | gauge | Average read response time (microseconds). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_total_read_transfer_rate | gauge | Sum of random and sequential read transfer rate for volume (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_random_read_transfer_rate | gauge | Random read transfer rate for volume (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_sequential_read_transfer_rate | gauge | Sequential read transfer rate for volume (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_total_write_transfer_rate | gauge | Sum of random and sequential write transfer rate for volume (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_random_write_transfer_rate | gauge | Random write transfer rate for volume (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_sequential_write_transfer_rate | gauge | Sequential write transfer rate for volume (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_volume_read_cache_hit_rate | gauge | Read cache hit rate for volume. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id |
| spc_pod_volume_total_capacity | gauge | Total volume capacity, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_used_capacity | gauge | Used volume capacity, grouped by pod name. Verify capacity from OS as well. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_free_capacity | gauge | Free volume capacity, grouped by pod name. Verify capacity from OS as well. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_used_capacity_rate | gauge | Used volume capacity rate, grouped by pod name. Verify capacity from OS as well. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_total_read_iops | gauge | Sum of random and sequential read IOPS for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_random_read_iops | gauge | Random read IOPS for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_sequential_read_iops | gauge | Sequential read IOPS for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_total_write_iops | gauge | Sum of random and sequential write IOPS for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_random_write_iops | gauge | Random write IOPS for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_sequential_write_iops | gauge | Sequential write IOPS for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_average_write_response_time | gauge | Average write response time for volume, grouped by pod name (microseconds). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_average_read_response_time | gauge | Average read response time for volume, grouped by pod name (microseconds). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_total_read_transfer_rate | gauge | Sum of random and sequential read transfer rate for volume, grouped by pod name (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_random_read_transfer_rate | gauge | Random read transfer rate for volume, grouped by pod name (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_sequential_read_transfer_rate | gauge | Sequential read transfer rate for volume, grouped by pod name (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_total_write_transfer_rate | gauge | Sum of random and sequential write transfer rate for volume, grouped by pod name (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_random_write_transfer_rate | gauge | Random write transfer rate for volume, grouped by pod name (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_sequential_write_transfer_rate | gauge | Sequential write transfer rate for volume, grouped by pod name (bytes/sec). | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_pod_volume_read_cache_hit_rate | gauge | Read cache hit rate for volume, grouped by pod name. | persistentvolume, persistentvolumeclaim, namespace, storageclass, serial_id, ldev_id_dec, ldev_id_hex, pool_id, pod |
| spc_sc_pool_used_capacity_rate | gauge | Used pool capacity rate. | storageclass, serial_id, pool_id |
| spc_sc_pool_total_capacity | gauge | Total capacity of storage pool (MiB). | storageclass, serial_id, pool_id |
| spc_sc_pool_free_capacity | gauge | Free capacity of storage pool (MiB). | storageclass, serial_id, pool_id |
| spc_sc_pool_used_capacity | gauge | Used capacity of storage pool (MiB). | storageclass, serial_id, pool_id |




---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/troubleshooting.md
---

# Troubleshooting

## Logs and Diagnostic Information

| Log / Information                 | Description                                                           | Command                                                                                           |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster info | Version, nodes, logs                                                  | `kubectl cluster-info dump -A > dump.txt`                                                         |
| HSPP version                      | Listed at the head of the `storage-exporter` log file                 | See storage-exporter log                                                                          |
| `storage-exporter` log            | Verify Deployment logs                                                | `kubectl logs -n hspc-monitoring-system <storage-exporter-pod>`                                   |
| Pod/PVC/StorageClass YAML         | Objects not collecting data                                           | `kubectl get pod -o yaml <Pod>` / `kubectl get pvc -o yaml <PVC>` / `kubectl get sc -o yaml <SC>` |
| HSPP config file                  | Check `storage-exporter.yaml` in Secret `storage-exporter-secret`     | `kubectl get secret storage-exporter-secret -n hspc-monitoring-system -o yaml`                    |
| HSPC version                      | See [HSPC Quick Reference Guide](/container-plugins/hspc/index.md)    | —                                                                                                 |
| HSPC controller logs              | `hspc-csi-controller` logs                                            | See [HSPC Quick Reference Guide](/container-plugins/hspc/index.md)                                |
| HSPC node logs                    | `hspc-csi-node` logs                                                  | See [HSPC Quick Reference Guide](/container-plugins/hspc/index.md)                                |
| Storage dump log                  | SVP normal dump (if using SVP) or system dump via maintenance utility | See System Administrator Guide                                                                    |

## Troubleshooting Memory Limit Issues

**Symptom:** The `storage-exporter` or `pushgateway` pod status shows `OOMKilled`.

**Solution:** Adjust the memory limits for the affected Deployment.

See [Kubernetes: Assign Memory Resources to Containers](https://kubernetes.io/docs/tasks/configure-pod-container/assign-memory-resource/) for configuration instructions.

## Troubleshooting Data Collection Issues \{#data-collection-issues}

**Symptom:** Data does not refresh every 5 minutes, or IOPS/Transfer Rate/Cache Hit Rate metrics become unavailable.

**Root cause:** Data collection time exceeds the collection interval. This depends primarily on the number of PersistentVolumeClaims (PVCs). If collection takes more than **11 minutes** in large-scale environments, IOPS, Transfer Rate, and Cache Hit Rate data become unavailable — HSPP cannot guarantee the calculation results for these metrics.

**Solutions:**

1. **Extend the collection interval** using the `SPC_INTERVAL` environment variable (valid range: 300–600 seconds):

   ```bash
   kubectl set env deployment/storage-exporter \
     -n hspc-monitoring-system \
     SPC_INTERVAL=600
   ```

2. **Remove unused PVCs** to reduce the number of resources HSPP must collect data for.

:::note
Data refresh happens every five minutes by default. The `SPC_INTERVAL` setting extends this interval but does not affect the frequency at which Prometheus scrapes the Pushgateway.
:::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/release-notes.md
---

# HSPP v3.17.4 Release Notes

**Document:** RN-92ADPTR154-07 (February 2026)

This document provides late-breaking information about Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Prometheus v3.17.4. It includes information not available when the technical documentation was published, as well as known issues and solutions.

:::note
This is a **minor release** that adds support for VSP One Block High End storage systems. The version number has been updated to 3.17.x to maintain consistency with Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers versioning.
:::

## About This Release

For the most current documentation and downloads:

- **Docs:** [docs.hitachivantara.com](https://docs.hitachivantara.com)
- **Downloads:** [support.hitachivantara.com](https://support.hitachivantara.com)
- **Compatibility:** [compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/hrpc](https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/hrpc/)

## Supported Software

| Software                               | Supported Version |
| -------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers | v3.17.4           |
| Kubernetes                             | 1.31 – 1.33       |
| Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform   | 4.18 – 4.20       |
| Prometheus v2.x                        | v2.24.1 or later  |
| Prometheus Pushgateway v1.x            | v1.4.0 or later   |

**Supported Operating Systems:**

- Red Hat Enterprise Linux 8.2–8.4, 8.6–8.8, 8.10, 9.0, 9.2–9.4, 9.6
- Red Hat Enterprise Linux Core OS 4.18–4.20

:::note
NVMe over Fibre Channel is supported only on Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.x and Red Hat Enterprise Linux Core OS.
:::

## Supported Storage Systems

| Storage System                                     | Minimum Firmware                                    | Protocol                                      |
| -------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| VSP One Block High End                             | A0-05-20-00/05 or later                             | Fibre Channel                                 |
| VSP One B24, B26, B28                              | A3-04-20 or later                                   | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over Fibre Channel |
| VSP 5100, 5500, 5100H, 5500H                       | 90-04-04 or later                                   | Fibre Channel, iSCSI                          |
| VSP 5200, 5600, 5200H, 5600H                       | 90-08-01 or later (NVMe over FC requires 90-09-21+) | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over Fibre Channel |
| VSP E1090, E1090H                                  | 93-06-01 or later (NVMe over FC requires 93-07-21+) | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over Fibre Channel |
| VSP E590, E790, E990, E590H, E790H                 | 93-02-04 or later                                   | Fibre Channel, iSCSI                          |
| VSP G350, G370, G700, G900, F350, F370, F700, F900 | 88-06-02 or later                                   | Fibre Channel, iSCSI                          |

## New Features and Enhancements in v3.17.4

- **VSP One Block High End support** — Added Fibre Channel support for VSP One Block High End storage systems.
- **Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9.6 support** added.
- **Kubernetes 1.32 and 1.33** support added.
- **Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform 4.18, 4.19, and 4.20** support added.

## Restrictions and Considerations

- In version 1.2.0 and later, the prefix `spc_` is attached to all metric names by default. To use metric names from version 1.1.0 or earlier, set the `SPC_DISABLE_METRIC_NAME_PREFIX` environment variable of the `storage-exporter` Deployment to `true`. See [Configuration](/container-plugins/hspp/v3.17.4/configuration.md#environment-variables).
- **NVMe over Fibre Channel is not supported.**
- **ReadOnlyMany is not supported.**
- When using HTTPS with certificate verification enabled, the certificate must be installed into the HSPP container image.

## Technology Preview

Technology Preview features provide early access to upcoming innovations for testing only. They are not covered by support plans, may not be functionally complete, and are not intended for production use.

## Getting Help

- **Support:** [support.hitachivantara.com](https://support.hitachivantara.com/en_us/contact-us.html)
- **Community:** [community.hitachivantara.com](https://community.hitachivantara.com)
- **Doc feedback:** [doc.feedback@hitachivantara.com](mailto:doc.feedback@hitachivantara.com) (include document number RN-92ADPTR154-07 and revision level)
