HSPC is deployed on OpenShift using the certified Operator from **OperatorHub**.

:::note Disconnected environments
To install in an OpenShift environment without internet access, mirror the `certified-operators` catalog in advance. See the [OpenShift documentation](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/latest/disconnected/mirroring/installing-mirroring-installation-images.html) for details. For example, for OCP 4.10 the index image is `registry.redhat.io/redhat/certified-operator-index:v4.10`.
:::

### Procedure

1. Access **OperatorHub** from the OpenShift web console.

2. Search for **Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers** and install the Operator.

   :::note Operator subscription settings

   - **Installation mode:** Select _A specific namespace on the cluster_ and specify your target namespace.
   - **Update approval:** Select _Manual_ and approve the Install Plan.

   :::

3. Confirm the Operator status is **Succeeded**.

4. Confirm the Operator Pod status is **Running**.

5. Click **Create Instance** on the Operator Details page.

6. Click **Create**. For advanced configuration, see [Configuring HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/configuration.md).

7. Confirm HSPC is ready:

```bash
oc get hspc -n <hspc-namespace>
NAME    READY    AGE
hspc    true     30s
```

## Installation on Kubernetes

For Kubernetes (and RKE2/EKS), HSPC is deployed using the operator manifests from the [csi-operator-hitachi](https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi) GitHub repository.

### Procedure

1. Clone the repository and check out the target version:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/operator
```

2. Create the Operator namespace and verify it was created:

```bash
kubectl create -f hspc-operator-namespace.yaml
kubectl get namespaces | grep hspc-operator-system
# Expected: hspc-operator-system   Active   53s
```

3. Deploy the Operator and confirm it is running:

```bash
kubectl create -f hspc-operator.yaml
kubectl get deployment -n hspc-operator-system
# Expected:
# NAME                              READY  UP-TO-DATE  AVAILABLE   AGE
# hspc-operator-controller-manager  1/1    1           1           14s
```

4. (Optional) Edit `hspc_v1_hspc.yaml` to customize the HSPC instance settings. See [Configuring HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/configuration.md) for available parameters.

5. Create the HSPC instance and confirm it is ready:

```bash
kubectl create -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
kubectl get hspc -n <hspc-namespace>
# Expected:
# NAME   READY   AGE
# hspc   true    30s
```

Replace `<hspc-namespace>` with the namespace specified in `hspc_v1_hspc.yaml`.

:::tip
Verify the namespace value is consistent across all plugin configurations and operations.
:::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/overview.md
---

# Overview

Version: 3.16.1
Chapter: 1

Storage Plug-in for Containers is software for creating and managing Persistent Volumes for Hitachi storage systems in a Kubernetes environment. It integrates Kubernetes or OpenShift with Hitachi storage systems using the **Container Storage Interface (CSI)**.

## Architecture

HSPC consists of two core components deployed in your cluster:

| Component        | Type                       | Purpose                                                                                                                                                              |
| ---------------- | -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `csi-controller` | Deployment (control plane) | Implements the CSI controller service; uses the storage REST API for provisioning, snapshots, and replication. Falls back to a node if control plane is unavailable. |
| `csi-node`       | DaemonSet (all nodes)      | Implements the CSI node service; manages volume attachment and mounting on each node. All nodes must run this component.                                             |

The `csi-controller` communicates with the storage system over the REST API. The `csi-node` handles the data path (FC, iSCSI, NVMe over FC, NVMe/TCP) from each Kubernetes node to the storage.

## Environment Setup Tasks

To use HSPC, complete these steps in order:

1. Verify server, storage, Kubernetes/OpenShift, and network requirements
2. Set up the Kubernetes or OpenShift environment
3. Configure supported storage systems using the appropriate protocol
4. Install HSPC

## Requirements

### Container Orchestrators

| Orchestrator                         | Supported Versions     | Notes                                                |
| ------------------------------------ | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform | 4.17, 4.18, 4.19       | —                                                    |
| Kubernetes                           | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 | —                                                    |
| Rancher Kubernetes Engine 2 (RKE2)   | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 | References to Kubernetes in this guide apply to RKE2 |
| Amazon EKS                           | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 | References to Kubernetes in this guide apply to EKS  |

For the latest supported versions, see the Release Notes.

### Server Requirements

| Component                    | Requirement                                  |
| ---------------------------- | -------------------------------------------- |
| CPU                          | x86\_64                                      |
| Operating system             | See Release Notes for supported OS versions  |
| Interface (bare metal)       | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC, NVMe/TCP |
| Interface (virtual machines) | iSCSI, NVMe/TCP                              |

### Storage Requirements (VSP Family and VSP One Block)

| Component    | Requirement                                                                                                                |
| ------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Host group   | Created automatically by HSPC using predefined naming rules. Do not use host groups created by HSPC for any other purpose. |
| User account | Must be in the built-in **Storage administrator (View & Modify)** user group, or a customized group with equivalent roles. |
| License      | Dynamic Provisioning (DP), Hitachi Thin Image (HTI), Hitachi Thin Image Advanced (HTIA) for VSP One Block                  |
| SVP          | Single and dual SVP configurations are supported. Not required for VSP One Block.                                          |
| Interface    | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, and NVMe over FC                                                                                     |

:::note
NVMe/TCP is not supported for VSP family and VSP One Block.
:::

### Storage Requirements (VSP One SDS Block)

| Component                        | Requirement                                                                      |
| -------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Version                          | Refer to the release notes for details.                                          |
| Interface                        | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, and NVMe/TCP                                               |
| User account (no multitenancy)   | Must be assigned the Storage role.                                               |
| User account (with multitenancy) | See [Multitenancy Functionality Settings](#multitenancy-functionality-settings). |

:::note
NVMe/TCP is supported only for VSP One SDS Block 01.16.00.4x.
:::

### Network Requirements

- HSPC uses storage **port 80 or 443** for REST API connections.
- **IPv6 is not supported.** Use IPv4.

## Pre-Installation Tasks

### Server Pre-Installation

Before installing HSPC, apply the following server configuration:

#### Multipath Settings

HSPC requires multipath to be configured on each node for redundant storage connectivity.

**Device Mapper Multipath (FC, iSCSI)**

For Kubernetes nodes, install and configure `multipathd`. A sample configuration file is available in the [csi-operator-hitachi](https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi) repository.

For OpenShift, apply a `MachineConfig` resource:

```bash
# Apply multipath MachineConfig
oc apply -f multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml
```

The sample `multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml` embeds a `multipath.conf` encoded as base64:

```bash
# Encode your multipath.conf
cat multipath-sample.conf | base64 -w0
```

:::note
HSPC does not support IQNs that include uppercase alphabetic characters.
:::

#### Native NVMe Multipath Settings

For NVMe over FC or NVMe/TCP connections, enable native NVMe multipath on each node. For OpenShift:

```bash
oc apply -f multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml
```

:::note
Do not change a host NQN while the host is running. Perform the drain operation on the node first, then change the host NQN. After changing the host NQN, restart the host. When there are multiple nodes, make sure that no duplicate host NQN exists across nodes.
:::

Refer to the `csi-operator-hitachi` repository for the NVMe-specific MachineConfig sample.

### Storage Pre-Installation — VSP Family and VSP One Block

Before installing HSPC, configure the storage system:

1. Create a **storage system user account** with the Storage administrator (View & Modify) role.
2. Ensure the required licenses are applied: Dynamic Provisioning (DP), Hitachi Thin Image (HTI), and HTIA (VSP One Block only).
3. Set up host groups and ports as required by your connection type.

#### Host Group and iSCSI Target Naming Rules

HSPC creates host groups and iSCSI targets using the following naming convention:

```
hspc-<cluster-id>-<node-id>
```

- `<cluster-id>`: Derived from the Kubernetes cluster UID
- `<node-id>`: Derived from the node UID

Do not manually create or modify host groups that match this naming pattern. Do not use HSPC-managed host groups for any other storage workloads.

### Storage Pre-Installation — VSP One SDS Block

:::note
Do not add a WWN, IQN, or NQN of multiple hosts to the same Server resource. Only one Server resource can be associated with a single host.
:::

#### Multitenancy Functionality Settings

If you use the VSP One SDS Block multitenancy functionality (Virtual Private Storage, VPS):

:::note
In an environment where one Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster uses one VSP One SDS Block, the following restrictions apply to Virtual Private Storage (VPS): Multiple VPSs cannot be used. VPS and resources that do not belong to the VPS cannot be used at the same time.
:::

1. Create a VPS for HSPC use. For the maximum number of compute nodes set for VPS, set a number greater than the number of nodes in the Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster. For the maximum number of sessions, set at least 20.
2. Create a user group for HSPC. For the scope, set only the created VPS. For the role, set **VpsStorage**. Do not assign the users to a user group other than the user group you created in this step.
3. Create a storage system user and assign it to the user group from step 2.
4. Specify the VPS ID in the Secret (`vpsID` parameter).

Refer to the VSP One SDS Block Storage Administrator Guide for detailed multitenancy setup procedures.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/provisioning.md
---

# Provisioning Volumes

Version: 3.16.1
Chapter: 3 (Usage)

## PersistentVolumeClaim Settings

A PVC requests storage from a StorageClass. HSPC dynamically provisions a new LDEV (or SDS Block volume) when the PVC is created.

:::note
To use an existing storage volume as a PVC, see [Static Provisioning](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/appendix.md#static-provisioning).

If you use both dynamic PVC provisioning and static provisioning in the same environment, verify that all static PVs have `claimRef` correctly set before creating new dynamic PVCs. PVs with `STATUS: Available` and no `claimRef` may be incorrectly bound to dynamically-created PVCs instead of creating a new volume. To check:

1. Check for unbound static PVs: `kubectl get pv` — PVs with `STATUS: Available` are not yet associated.
2. For each available PV, check if `claimRef` is set: `kubectl get pv <PV-name> -o yaml`
3. If any PV has no `claimRef`, either re-create it with `claimRef` specified and then create the matching PVC, or delete it if it is no longer needed. If you proceed without fixing the `claimRef`, HSPC will not create a new dynamic PV and the PVC may bind to the incomplete static PV.
   :::

### PVC YAML Reference (`pvc-sample.yaml`)

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample               # (1) PVC name
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce                # (2) Access mode
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi               # (3) Volume size
  storageClassName: sc-sample    # (4) StorageClass name
```

| # | Field              | Description                                                                                                                                                           |
| - | ------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1 | `name`             | PVC name                                                                                                                                                              |
| 2 | `accessModes`      | `ReadWriteOnce`, `ReadOnlyMany`, or `ReadWriteMany`. For ROX, see [ReadOnlyMany](#readonlymany). For RWX or block volumes, see [Raw Block Volume](#raw-block-volume). |
| 3 | `storage`          | Requested volume size                                                                                                                                                 |
| 4 | `storageClassName` | Must match an existing StorageClass                                                                                                                                   |

### PVC Failure Handling

- **Creation failure:** A PVC object is created without a bound PV. Delete the PVC with `kubectl delete pvc <name>` and retry.
- **Deletion failure:** The PVC object is deleted, but the PV and storage asset may remain. Locate the LDEV via `kubectl get pv` → `spec.csi.volumeHandle`, then delete the PV with `kubectl delete pv <name>` and the LDEV via storage management software.

***

## Pod Settings

The Pod manifest references a PVC via a volume claim, and HSPC handles the mount.

### Pod YAML Reference (`pod-sample.yaml`)

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: pod-sample               # (1) Pod name
spec:
  containers:
    - name: my-busybox
      image: busybox
      volumeMounts:
      - mountPath: "/data"       # (2) Mount path inside the container
        name: sample-volume
      command: ["sleep", "1000000"]
      imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  volumes:
    - name: sample-volume
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: pvc-sample    # (3) PVC name
```

***

## Command Examples

:::note OpenShift
Replace `kubectl` with `oc` in all commands.
:::

### Create Resources

```bash
# Create the Secret
kubectl create -f secret-sample.yaml
kubectl get secret
# NAME            TYPE     DATA   AGE
# secret-sample   Opaque   3      34s

# Create the StorageClass
kubectl create -f sc-sample.yaml
kubectl get sc
# NAME        PROVISIONER            AGE
# sc-sample   hspc.csi.hitachi.com   21s

# Create the PVC
kubectl create -f pvc-sample.yaml
kubectl get pvc
# NAME         STATUS   VOLUME                                     CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   STORAGECLASS   AGE
# pvc-sample   Bound    pvc-cf8c6089-0386-4c39-8037-e1520a986a7d   1Gi        RWO            sc-sample      28s

# Create the Pod
kubectl create -f pod-sample.yaml
kubectl get pod
# NAME         READY   STATUS    RESTARTS   AGE
# pod-sample   1/1     Running   0          20s
```

:::caution
Do not change the LDEV nickname after it has been created by HSPC.
:::

### Inspect the PersistentVolume

```bash
kubectl get pv
kubectl describe pv pvc-3796f902-ed64-4636-9d25-e73e28e556f2
```

Example `describe` output shows storage details in `spec.csi.volumeAttributes`:

```
VolumeHandle: 01--scsi--900000070010--914--spc-ecdf100f22
VolumeAttributes:
  connectionType=fc
  ldevIDDec=914
  ldevIDHex=03:92
  nickname=spc-ecdf100f22
  ports=CL5-C
  size=1Gi
```

### Delete Resources

```bash
kubectl delete pod pod-sample
kubectl delete pvc pvc-sample
kubectl delete sc sc-sample
kubectl delete secret secret-sample
```

***

## Raw Block Volume

Kubernetes supports raw block volumes in addition to filesystem volumes. Use raw block mode when the application needs direct device access.

### Raw Block PVC (`pvc-sample-block.yaml`)

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample-block
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce         # ReadWriteOnce or ReadWriteMany
  volumeMode: Block       # Required for raw block
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
```

### Raw Block Pod (`pod-sample-block.yaml`)

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: pod-sample-block
spec:
  containers:
    - name: my-busybox
      image: busybox
      volumeDevices:
      - devicePath: "/block"       # Device path inside the container
        name: sample-volume
      command: ["sleep", "1000000"]
      imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  volumes:
    - name: sample-volume
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: pvc-sample-block
```

```bash
kubectl create -f pvc-sample-block.yaml
kubectl create -f pod-sample-block.yaml
```

***

## ReadWriteMany

ReadWriteMany (RWX) allows a volume to be mounted on multiple nodes simultaneously with read-write access. This is primarily used for **OpenShift Virtualization**.

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample-rwx
spec:
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteMany
  volumeMode: Block
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 7Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
```

***

## ReadOnlyMany

ReadOnlyMany (ROX) mounts a volume on multiple nodes for read-only access. A ROX PVC must be created **from an existing PVC** (clone-based).

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample-rox
spec:
  dataSource:
    name: pvc-sample           # Source PVC
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    apiGroup: ""
  accessModes:
  - ReadOnlyMany               # Specify ReadOnlyMany
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/snapshots.md
---

# Volume Snapshots

Version: 3.16.1
Chapter: 3 (Usage)

## Cloning a PVC

Cloning creates a duplicate of an existing volume. A clone can be used in the same way as any standard volume.

:::note

- Cloning is **not supported** for VSP One B20 or VSP One SDS Block.
- If the source volume was recently expanded, confirm the expansion is complete before cloning.
- Flush data before cloning for consistency (e.g., remove the Pod temporarily).

:::

**Prerequisites:** StorageClass and an existing PersistentVolumeClaim.

### Clone PVC YAML (`pvc-from-pvc-sample.yaml`)

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-from-pvc-sample        # (1) Clone PVC name
spec:
  dataSource:
    name: pvc-sample               # (2) Source PVC name
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
    apiGroup: ""
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi                 # (3) Must match the source volume size
  storageClassName: sc-sample      # (4) Same StorageClass as the source
```

:::note Size
Use `kubectl get pv <pv-name> -o yaml` to check the `size` field. For expanded or statically provisioned volumes, use `kubectl get pv <pv-name>` and look at the `CAPACITY` column.
:::

```bash
# Create the clone
kubectl create -f pvc-from-pvc-sample.yaml
```

***

## Creating a Snapshot for a PVC

Volume snapshots create a point-in-time image of a PVC. Snapshots can be used to restore or duplicate a previous state.

:::note

- Snapshots are **not supported** on VSP One SDS Block.
- If the volume was recently expanded, confirm the expansion is complete before snapshotting.
- Flush data before snapshotting (e.g., remove the Pod temporarily).
- For Kubernetes environments, install Snapshot CRDs and Snapshot Controller per cluster from [external-snapshotter](https://github.com/kubernetes-csi/external-snapshotter). Use **v1** CRDs. Remove Alpha/Beta CRDs if present.

:::

### VolumeSnapshotClass YAML

```yaml
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshotClass
metadata:
  name: snapshotclass-sample       # (1)
driver: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
deletionPolicy: Delete
parameters:
  poolID: "1"                      # (2) Same poolID as the StorageClass
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-name: "secret-sample"       # (3)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-namespace: "default"        # (4)
```

### VolumeSnapshot YAML

```yaml
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshot
metadata:
  name: snapshot-sample                          # (1)
spec:
  volumeSnapshotClassName: snapshotclass-sample  # (2)
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: pvc-sample        # (3) Source PVC
```

### Restore Snapshot to a New PVC

:::note
Restoring a PVC from a snapshot is **not supported** on VSP One B20.
:::

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-from-snapshot-sample
spec:
  dataSource:
    name: snapshot-sample            # VolumeSnapshot name
    kind: VolumeSnapshot
    apiGroup: snapshot.storage.k8s.io
  accessModes:
  - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi                   # Must match source volume size
  storageClassName: sc-sample
```

### Snapshot Commands

```bash
# Create the VolumeSnapshotClass
kubectl create -f volumesnapshotclass-sample.yaml

# Create a VolumeSnapshot
kubectl create -f volumesnapshot-sample.yaml

# Verify the snapshot is ready (readyToUse: true)
kubectl get volumesnapshot -o yaml

# Restore from snapshot
kubectl create -f pvc-from-snapshot-sample.yaml
```

If `readyToUse` is `false`:

```bash
# Get the VolumeSnapshotContent name
kubectl get volumesnapshot -o yaml

# Check for error messages
kubectl describe volumesnapshotcontent <VolumeSnapshotContentName>
```

***

## Snapshots for a Stretched PVC

Creating a snapshot for a Stretched PVC requires a dedicated `VolumeSnapshotClass` that includes the `serialNumber` parameter to identify the storage system.

```yaml title="volumesnapshotclass-stretched-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshotClass
metadata:
  name: snapshotclass-stretched-sample
driver: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
deletionPolicy: Delete
parameters:
  poolID: "1"                                                            # Same poolID as the StorageClass
  serialNumber: "715035"                                                 # Required for Stretched PVC snapshots
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-namespace: "default"
```

Then create the `VolumeSnapshot` using this class, pointing to the Stretched PVC:

```yaml title="volumesnapshot-stretched-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshot
metadata:
  name: snapshot-stretched-sample
spec:
  volumeSnapshotClassName: snapshotclass-stretched-sample
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: <stretched-pvc-name>
```

See [Storage Classes — Stretched PVC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/storage-classes.md#stretched-pvc-global-active-device) for Stretched PVC setup.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/storage-classes.md
---

# Storage Classes

Version: 3.16.1
Chapter: 3 (Usage)

## Secret Settings

The Secret file contains the storage URL, user name, password, and host mode options required for HSPC to communicate with your storage system.

Sample files are available in the `csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/sample` directory of the [csi-operator-hitachi](https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi) repository.

### Secret YAML Reference (`secret-sample.yaml`)

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample              # (1)
type: Opaque
data:
  url: aHR0cDovLzE3Mi4xNi4xLjE=    # (2) base64-encoded storage URL
  user: VXNlcjAx                   # (3) base64-encoded username
  password: UGFzc3dvcmQwMQ==       # (4) base64-encoded password
  hostModeOptions: ODgsODENCg==    # (5) base64-encoded host mode options
```

| Field             | Description                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `url`             | base64-encoded storage URL. For VSP E/F350/F370/F700/F900/G350/G370/G700/G900: use the storage controller IP. For VSP 5000 series, F400/F600/F800/F1500, G200/G400/G600/G800/G1000/G1500, N400/N600/N800: use the SVP IP. For VSP One B20: use the service IP. |
| `user`            | base64-encoded storage user name. Example: `echo -n "User01" \| base64`                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `password`        | base64-encoded storage password. Example: `echo -n "Password01" \| base64`                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| `hostModeOptions` | base64-encoded host mode options. Example: `echo -n "88,81" \| base64`. Default options: `2, 22, 25, 68, 91`. Additional options specified here are added. Pod must be recreated to apply new options.                                                         |

## StorageClass Settings

The StorageClass file contains storage settings that are necessary for Storage Plug-in for Containers to work with your environment. The following samples provide information about the required parameters.

:::warning
After creating a StorageClass and PVC, re-creating the StorageClass will **not** affect existing PVCs.
:::

### StorageClass for VSP Family and VSP One Block

```yaml
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample                                                          # (1)
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"                                                    # (2)
  poolID: "1"                                                              # (3)
  portID: CL1-A,CL2-A                                                     # (4)
  connectionType: fc                                                       # (5)
  storageEfficiency: "CompressionDeduplication"                            # (6)
  storageEfficiencyMode: "PostProcess"                                     # (7)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4                                         # (8)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"            # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"             # (10)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"             # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"              # (10)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"      # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"       # (10)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"              # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"               # (10)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"       # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"        # (10)
```

| #  | Parameter                   | Description                                                                                                                                                                      |
| -- | --------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1  | `name`                      | StorageClass name                                                                                                                                                                |
| 2  | `serialNumber`              | Storage system serial number                                                                                                                                                     |
| 3  | `poolID`                    | HDP pool ID                                                                                                                                                                      |
| 4  | `portID`                    | Port ID(s), comma-separated for multipath. Not required for NVMe over FC.                                                                                                        |
| 5  | `connectionType`            | `fc`, `iscsi`, or `nvme-fc`. Default: `fc`. For NVMe over FC, also add `nvmSubsystemID`.                                                                                         |
| 6  | `storageEfficiency`         | Adaptive data reduction: `Compression`, `CompressionDeduplication`, or `Disabled` (default). For VSP One B20, `Disabled` is not supported (default: `CompressionDeduplication`). |
| 7  | `storageEfficiencyMode`     | `Inline` or `PostProcess`. Only valid when `storageEfficiency` is not `Disabled`. Default depends on storage model.                                                              |
| 8  | `csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype` | Filesystem type: `ext4` (default) or `xfs`.                                                                                                                                      |
| 9  | Secret name references      | Must match the name of your Secret resource.                                                                                                                                     |
| 10 | Secret namespace references | Must match the namespace of your Secret resource.                                                                                                                                |

:::caution
Do not change adaptive data reduction parameters (`storageEfficiency`, `storageEfficiencyMode`) on LDEVs created by HSPC.
:::

### StorageClass for VSP One SDS Block

```yaml
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample-vsp-one-sds-block
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  storageType: vsp-one-sds-block          # (1) Required for SDS Block
  connectionType: fc                      # (2) fc, iscsi, or nvme-tcp
  storageEfficiency: Disabled             # (3) Compression or Disabled
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4         # (4)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```

Note: If multitenancy is used, `storageEfficiency` cannot be set — data reduction follows VPS settings.

***

## Resource Partitioning

:::note
Resource partitioning is not supported on VSP One B20 or VSP One SDS Block.
:::

Resource partitioning allows you to isolate storage system resources per Kubernetes cluster. Use cases include:

- Restricting the range of LDEV IDs assigned to a resource group for a specific cluster
- Preventing impact between Kubernetes clusters that share the same storage system

### Supported Configuration

Multiple Kubernetes clusters can each use their own resource group on the same storage system:

- Each cluster uses a dedicated resource group with isolated LDEVs, host groups, and pools
- StorageClass and Secret are configured per resource group, per cluster

### Unsupported Configurations

**Example 1 — Mixed resource group and meta resource in one cluster**

You cannot have both of the following in the same Kubernetes cluster:

- A StorageClass and Secret configured for a specific resource group
- A StorageClass and Secret configured for the meta resource (default)

**Example 2 — Multiple resource groups per storage system in one cluster**

If a single storage system has multiple resource groups, only **one resource group per storage system** can be mapped to a given Kubernetes cluster.

### Storage System Requirements

| Resource       | Requirement                                                                                                                                            |
| -------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Resource group | Only one resource group per storage system per cluster.                                                                                                |
| User account   | Storage system user must have access only to the target resource group — no access to other groups.                                                    |
| Pool           | Create from pool volumes within the target resource group.                                                                                             |
| LDEV IDs       | Allocate sufficient unused LDEVs. If adaptive data reduction is enabled, also allocate LDEVs for deduplication system data volumes.                    |
| Host groups    | For each port, allocate one host group ID per host (e.g., 3 hosts × 2 ports = 6 host group IDs). Assign host group IDs to the resource group per port. |
| NVM subsystem  | For NVMe over FC: assign the NVM subsystem to the resource group.                                                                                      |
| Ports          | For NVMe over FC: assign ports to the resource group.                                                                                                  |

### Secret for Resource Partitioning

Add `resourceGroupID` to the Secret:

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample
type: Opaque
data:
  url: aHR0cDovLzE3Mi4xNi4xLjE=
  user: VXNlcjAx
  password: UGFzc3dvcmQwMQ==
stringData:
  resourceGroupID: "1"    # Specify the resource group ID
```

### StorageClass for Resource Partitioning (iSCSI)

For iSCSI connections, add the port IP addresses in order:

```yaml
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"
  poolID: "1"
  portID: CL1-A,CL2-A
  connectionType: iscsi
  portIP: "192.168.10.10, 192.168.10.11"    # Required for iSCSI resource partitioning
```

For Fibre Channel or NVMe over FC, no additional StorageClass parameters are required.

***

## Stretched PVC (Global Active Device)

The Stretched PVC feature automates provisioning of **synchronous active-active replication** between storage systems at two sites within a single Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster using **Hitachi Global Active Device (GAD)**.

This allows building a high-availability cluster where the primary and secondary sites each hold a fully synchronous copy of every provisioned volume.

:::note
Stretched PVC does not include management or monitoring of replication status or site failover. Use Storage Navigator or the GAD management tools for that.

:::note
Use **Element Manager for Block 20** to split or resync a GAD pair on VSP One Block storage systems.
:::
:::

### Requirements

**System-wide requirements:**

| Item      | Requirement                                                                                                                                |
| --------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Cluster   | A single stretched Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster spanning two sites, with one storage system allocated per site.                            |
| REST API  | HSPC must be able to communicate with both the primary and secondary storage systems via REST API.                                         |
| Data path | Each node must have data path connectivity to both storage systems. **Only Fibre Channel is supported** for the node-to-storage data path. |

**Storage system requirements (both sites):**

| Item                     | Requirement                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------ | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Supported models         | VSP One Block, VSP E series, VSP 5000 series, VSP F350/370/700/900, VSP G350/370/700/900                                                                                         |
| License                  | Global Active Device (GAD) license — see the GAD User Guide                                                                                                                      |
| Virtual storage machines | Create one VSM per storage system. Primary and secondary VSMs must have the same serial number and model. Use VSMs exclusively for HSPC. Do not use the VSMs for other purposes. |
| Users and user groups    | Create a user group with access limited to the VSM's resource group.                                                                                                             |
| Pool                     | Create from pool volumes within the VSM.                                                                                                                                         |
| LDEV IDs                 | Assign unused LDEV IDs to the VSM; set virtual LDEV IDs to unassigned (65534).                                                                                                   |
| Host groups              | Assign unused host group IDs for each port (minimum: total hosts + 1 per port).                                                                                                  |
| Remote paths             | Create remote paths. Primary and secondary must use the same path group ID.                                                                                                      |
| Quorum disks             | Create quorum disks. Primary and secondary must use the same quorum disk ID.                                                                                                     |

:::note
When using the same combination of storage systems from multiple Kubernetes/OpenShift clusters: use the same storage system users and virtual storage machines for each cluster, and verify that the primary-site and secondary-site storage system pairing is consistent across all clusters.
:::

**Compatibility with other features:**

| Feature                 | Compatible with Stretched PVC? |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------ |
| Volume snapshots        | Yes                            |
| Volume cloning          | Yes                            |
| Volume expansion        | Yes                            |
| Raw block volume        | Yes                            |
| ReadWriteMany           | Yes                            |
| ReadOnlyMany            | Yes                            |
| Adaptive data reduction | Yes                            |
| Static provisioning     | No                             |

### Creating a Stretched PVC

**Step 1 — Create the Stretched PVC Secret**

```yaml
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample-stretched
type: Opaque
stringData:
  primarySerial: "11111"                    # (1) Primary storage serial number
  primaryURL: http://172.16.0.1             # (2) Primary storage REST API URL
  primaryUser: primary-user                 # (3) Primary storage user
  primaryPassword: primary-password         # (4) Primary storage password
  secondarySerial: "22222"                  # (5) Secondary storage serial number
  secondaryURL: http://172.16.0.2           # (6) Secondary storage REST API URL
  secondaryUser: secondary-user             # (7) Secondary storage user
  secondaryPassword: secondary-password     # (8) Secondary storage password
```

**Step 2 — Create the Stretched PVC StorageClass**

```yaml
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample-stretched
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  connectionType: fc
  replicationType: stretched               # (1) Identifies this as a Stretched PVC StorageClass
  quorumID: "30"                           # (2) Quorum disk ID
  primaryPoolID: "10"                      # (3) Pool ID at primary site
  primaryPortID: CL1-A,CL2-A              # (4) Port IDs at primary site
  secondaryPoolID: "20"                    # (5) Pool ID at secondary site
  secondaryPortID: CL1-F                   # (6) Port IDs at secondary site
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample-stretched"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```

**Step 3 — Create the PVC**

Create a PVC referencing `sc-sample-stretched`. Stretched PVC creation takes longer than a regular PVC because GAD pair provisioning is performed.

**Step 4 — Verify**

```bash
kubectl get pvc
# STATUS should be Bound
```

### Checking Stretched PVC Details

After a Stretched PVC is created, inspect the PV for storage system resource details:

```bash
kubectl describe pv <pv-name>
```

Key attributes in `spec.csi.volumeAttributes`:

| Attribute           | Description                                   |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| `nickname`          | LDEV nickname (same at both sites)            |
| `primarySerial`     | Serial number of the primary storage system   |
| `primaryVolumeID`   | LDEV ID at the primary site                   |
| `secondarySerial`   | Serial number of the secondary storage system |
| `secondaryVolumeID` | LDEV ID at the secondary site                 |

### Adaptive Data Reduction with Stretched PVC

To enable adaptive data reduction on a Stretched PVC, add `storageEfficiency` and optionally `storageEfficiencyMode` to the StorageClass:

```yaml
parameters:
  replicationType: stretched
  storageEfficiency: "CompressionDeduplication"
  storageEfficiencyMode: "PostProcess"
  # ... other parameters
```

### Using Regular PVCs in a Stretched Cluster

To use a standard (non-GAD) PVC in a stretched cluster, configure resource partitioning first. Note:

- Create a resource group (not from a virtual storage machine).
- Use ports that are not used by Stretched PVCs.

Use Kubernetes **Node Affinity** or **Node Selector** to control which site the Pod is scheduled to.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/troubleshooting.md
---

# Troubleshooting

Version: 3.16.1
Chapter: 7

## Collecting Information for Support

When contacting Hitachi Vantara support, collect the following information:

| Information                | How to Collect                                                 |
| -------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Cluster information        | `kubectl cluster-info dump -A > dump.txt`                      |
| Command execution logs     | The command you ran and its output                             |
| `kubectl describe` output  | Run for the operation target resource                          |
| CSI controller logs        | See [Collecting HSPC Logs](#collecting-hspc-logs)              |
| CSI node logs              | See [Collecting HSPC Logs](#collecting-hspc-logs)              |
| PVC-related manifests      | YAML files for StorageClass, Secret, and PVC                   |
| Snapshot-related manifests | YAML files for VolumeSnapshotClass, Secret, and VolumeSnapshot |
| Snapshot-related logs      | Snapshot controller logs                                       |
| Application manifests      | YAML files for applications using HSPC PVCs                    |
| Storage logs               | See [Collecting Storage Logs](#collecting-storage-logs)        |

:::tip
For an optional **automated** log bundle (multiple pods, cluster context, and a browser-based viewer), see [Hitachi CSI Log Bundle](/container-plugins/csi-log-bundle.md). It is community-maintained and not a Hitachi Vantara product.
:::

## Collecting HSPC Logs

### Step 1 — Identify the Pod names

```bash
kubectl get pods -n <hspc-namespace>
```

### Step 2 — Retrieve logs

**Operator:**

```bash
kubectl logs -n <hspc-namespace> hspc-operator-controller-manager-<id>
```

**CSI controller:**

```bash
kubectl logs -n <hspc-namespace> hspc-csi-controller-<id> -c hspc-csi-driver
```

**CSI node** (repeat for each node — deployed as DaemonSet):

```bash
kubectl logs -n <hspc-namespace> hspc-csi-node-<id> -c hspc-csi-driver
```

### Step 3 — Collect rotated logs

Old logs may be rotated. To retrieve them:

1. From the `kubectl describe` output of the Pod, identify the target node.
2. SSH to the node.
3. Retrieve log files from the HSPC log directory.

## Collecting Storage Logs

### VSP Family and VSP One Block

If you are using an SVP, collect the regular dump files. If you are not using an SVP, collect system dumps using the maintenance utility. For details, see the System Administrator Guide.

### VSP One SDS Block

Use the VSP One SDS Block administration tools. Refer to the VSP One SDS Block Storage Administrator Guide.

***

## Viewing PersistentVolume Properties

When HSPC dynamically creates a volume, metadata is stored in `spec.csi.volumeAttributes` of the PersistentVolume. View with:

```bash
kubectl get pv <pv-name> -o yaml
```

### Properties for VSP Family and VSP One Block

| Property    | Description                                                                                                                                    |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `ldevIDDec` | Decimal LDEV ID                                                                                                                                |
| `ldevIDHex` | Hexadecimal LDEV ID                                                                                                                            |
| `size`      | Original capacity used when creating the volume (note: this value does not update after expansion — use `kubectl get pv` for current CAPACITY) |

### Properties for VSP One SDS Block

| Property   | Description                                                                                                                                    |
| ---------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `volumeID` | ID of the volume created in VSP One SDS Block                                                                                                  |
| `size`     | Original capacity used when creating the volume (note: this value does not update after expansion — use `kubectl get pv` for current CAPACITY) |

***

## Known Issues

### Forcibly Deleting a Pod

If you use `kubectl delete pod --force`, Kubernetes marks the Pod as deleted but the volume unmount may not complete. The deleted Pod and the information on the PVC associated with the Pod might remain on the relevant node, and an unexpected error might occur.

:::note
To properly delete this information, you must **restart the relevant node** before using the node again.
:::

### Creating and Deleting PVCs Simultaneously

Creating or deleting many PVCs at the same time can overload the storage system, producing errors:

- `0x0000100b`
- `0x0000100f`
- `0x0000101a`
- `0x0000f007`

Reduce the number of concurrent operations by lowering `--worker-threads` on the `csi-provisioner` container. See [Configuring HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/configuration.md#example-limiting-concurrent-worker-threads).

### Host Group Exhaustion / Error 0x00001023

If HSPC encounters error `0x00001023`, the storage has reached its host group limit or a host group name does not follow the HSPC naming format (`spc-<wwn1>-<wwn2>-<wwn3>`). To resolve:

1. Delete HSPC. See [Uninstalling HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/uninstalling.md).
2. Delete the host group specified in the error message.
3. Search for host groups that have WWNs for each host, and delete them or rename them to the `spc-<wwn1>-<wwn2>-<wwn3>` naming format.
4. Re-create HSPC. See [Installing HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/installation.md).

### Timeout Errors When Creating or Deleting Pods

If Pod creation or deletion times out:

1. Check CSI node logs for errors.
2. Verify the node has connectivity to the storage system on the configured ports.
3. Confirm multipath is configured correctly on the node.
4. If the timeout continues, re-create the HSPC instance. See [Installing HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/installation.md).
5. If the timeout still persists, restart the node to which the Pod is assigned.

:::caution
Restarting a node might affect other environments. Check the scope of the impact and follow the Kubernetes/OpenShift documentation procedures carefully.
:::

### Unable to Schedule or Create a Pod

If a Pod remains in Pending or ContainerCreating state, the issue may be that multipath services or modules are not configured correctly, or they are not running on the scheduled node.

1. Verify the multipath service status:

```bash
systemctl status multipathd
```

If the daemon is not running, continue to the next step.

2. Enable and start the multipath service:

```bash
systemctl enable --now multipathd
```

3. Load the multipath kernel module:

```bash
modprobe dm-multipath
```

4. Verify the multipath devices detected:

```bash
multipath -ll
```

Verify that the storage devices used by the PersistentVolume are listed.

:::note
You must reboot the node after configuring the multipath settings.
:::

If the issue persists, also check: `kubectl describe pod <pod-name>` and review the Events section, confirm the PVC is in `Bound` state, and verify the node has the required storage interface (FC HBA, iSCSI initiator, or NVMe adapter) configured.

### Stretched PVC — Host Failure

If a host fails, the status of the Pod run by the failed host becomes `Terminating` and the Pod enters an abnormal state. To resume the Pod on a healthy host:

1. Forcibly delete the Pod:

```bash
kubectl delete pod <Pod-name> --grace-period=0 --force
```

2. Delete the `VolumeAttachment` related to the Stretched PVC the Pod was using:

```bash
kubectl delete volumeattachment <VolumeAttachment-name>
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/uninstalling.md
---

# Upgrade & Uninstall

Version: 3.16.1
Chapters: 4, 5, 6

## Upgrading HSPC

:::warning Before upgrading

- Remove any **alpha VolumeSnapshotClass and VolumeSnapshot** resources before upgrading.
- If your OpenShift or Kubernetes version is older than the minimum required for the new HSPC version, upgrade the orchestrator first.
- Back up your HSPC instance configuration (`hspc_v1_hspc.yaml`) before upgrading — settings may be needed for the new version. If you do not have the file, run `kubectl get hspc -A -o yaml` to obtain the configured settings.

:::

### Upgrade on OpenShift

HSPC upgrades on OpenShift require a full delete-and-reinstall via the web console.

1. Delete the HSPC instance from the **Storage Plug-in for Containers** tab in Operator Details.
2. Uninstall the **Storage Plug-in for Containers** Operator from the OpenShift web console.
3. Install the new version of Storage Plug-in for Containers. See [Installing HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/installation.md).

### Upgrade on Kubernetes

For Kubernetes, upgrading is a delete-and-reinstall:

1. Delete HSPC and the Operator using the **previous version** manifests:

```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
kubectl delete -f hspc-operator.yaml
```

2. Install the new version. See [Installing HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/quick-reference/installation.md).

***

## Re-creating HSPC

Re-creation is used to reset the HSPC instance (e.g., after a configuration change that requires a full restart).

### Re-create on OpenShift

1. Access the OpenShift web console.
2. Delete the HSPC instance from the Operator Details page.
3. Create a new HSPC instance from the same Operator Details page.

### Re-create on Kubernetes

```bash
# Delete the instance
kubectl delete hspc -n <hspc-namespace> hspc

# Re-create the instance
kubectl create -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
```

Replace `<hspc-namespace>` with the namespace specified in `hspc_v1_hspc.yaml`.

***

## Uninstalling HSPC

Uninstallation removes all HSPC components from the cluster. This includes PVCs, PVs, StorageClasses, Secrets, and the Operator itself.

:::danger
Deleting PVCs and PVs is irreversible. Ensure you no longer need the data on those volumes before proceeding.
:::

### Uninstall on OpenShift

1. Delete all Pods that use volumes provisioned by HSPC.
2. Delete the following resources created for HSPC:
   - `VolumeSnapshotClass`
   - `VolumeSnapshot`
   - `PersistentVolumeClaim`
   - `StorageClass`
   - `Secret`
3. Delete the HSPC instance from the **Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers** tab in the Operator Details.
4. Uninstall the Operator from the OpenShift web console.

### Uninstall on Kubernetes

1. Delete all Pods that use HSPC-provisioned volumes.

2. Delete HSPC resources:

```bash
# Delete PVCs
kubectl delete pvc --all -n <app-namespace>

# Delete StorageClasses and Secrets
kubectl delete sc <sc-name>
kubectl delete secret <secret-name>

# Delete VolumeSnapshots and VolumeSnapshotClasses
kubectl delete volumesnapshot --all -n <app-namespace>
kubectl delete volumesnapshotclass <snapshotclass-name>
```

3. Delete the HSPC instance:

```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
```

4. Delete the Operator:

```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc-operator.yaml
```

5. Delete the HSPC namespace:

```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc-operator-namespace.yaml
```

6. If you created a Secret to pull the HSPC image, delete it:

```bash
kubectl delete secret <image-pull-secret-name> -n <hspc-namespace>
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.16.1/release-notes.md
---

# Release Notes — HSPC v3.16.1

Version: 3.16.1

:::note
Full release notes for HSPC 3.16.1 are published separately as **RN-92ADPTR141** (the corresponding release notes document for the 3.16.x line).

Please refer to that document for the complete list of new features, resolved issues, known issues, and upgrade requirements.
:::

## Highlights

The 3.16.x release line for HSPC introduced:

- **VSP One SDS Block** — software-defined block storage support via FC, iSCSI, and NVMe/TCP
- **Stretched PVC (GAD)** — active-active synchronous replication across two sites using Global Active Device
- **Resource partitioning** — per-cluster isolation of LDEV IDs, host groups, and pools
- **Static provisioning** — use existing storage volumes directly as PVCs
- **ReadWriteMany (RWX)** — block volume support for OpenShift Virtualization
- **ReadOnlyMany (ROX)** — multi-node read-only mounts from existing PVCs
- **NVMe over FC and NVMe/TCP** — NVMe connectivity support for bare metal nodes

## Supported Platforms

| Component         | Versions               |
| ----------------- | ---------------------- |
| Red Hat OpenShift | 4.17, 4.18, 4.19       |
| Kubernetes        | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 |
| RKE2              | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 |
| Amazon EKS        | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 |

## Upgrading to 3.17.x

For detailed upgrade instructions from 3.16.x to 3.17.x, refer to the [HSPC v3.17.4 documentation](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/installation.md).



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/index.md
---

# Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers (HSPC)




---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/appendix.md
---

# Appendix A: Advanced Configuration

## Editing the Existing LUN Configuration

You can modify the LUN configuration for existing PVs if you need to change port assignments or other storage-level settings.

:::warning
Modifying LUN configurations for active PVs can disrupt I/O. Plan maintenance windows accordingly.
:::

1. Identify the LDEV ID from the PV:
   ```bash
   kubectl describe pv <PV-name>
   ```
2. Use Storage Navigator or the REST API to modify the LUN configuration on the storage system.
3. Restart the affected Pods to reconnect with the new configuration.

## Migrating Existing Ports of a StorageClass

To migrate volumes to use different ports (for example, after hardware reconfiguration):

### Update the StorageClass with the new port IDs
Edit the `portID` parameter in the StorageClass YAML and apply:
```bash
kubectl apply -f sc-updated.yaml
```
:::note
Changes to the StorageClass apply to **new PVCs only** — existing PVCs are not affected.
:::
### For existing PVCs, create new PVCs and migrate data
There is no in-place migration of existing PV port assignments via Kubernetes. To move existing volumes to different ports:
1. Create new PVCs using the updated StorageClass.
2. Copy data from the old PVC to the new PVC.
3. Update application Pod manifests to use the new PVC.
4. Delete the old PVC when migration is complete.

## Modifying the Bulk Mapping Mode

Bulk mapping allows HSPC to map multiple LUNs simultaneously, improving performance during provisioning.

Configure the `bulkMapping` parameter in the instance CustomResource:

```yaml title="hspc_v1_hspc.yaml"
spec:
  controller:
    containers:
      - name: hspc-csi-driver
        env:
          - name: SPC_BULK_MAP_ENABLED
            value: "true"
```

## Modifying CPU and Memory Resources for the CSI Controller Deployment

Adjust resource requests and limits for the `hspc-csi-controller` Deployment:

```yaml title="hspc_v1_hspc.yaml"
spec:
  controller:
    containers:
      - name: hspc-csi-driver
        resources:
          requests:
            cpu: "250m"
            memory: "256Mi"
          limits:
            cpu: "1000m"
            memory: "512Mi"
```

Apply the changes by re-creating the HSPC instance.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/configuration.md
---

# Configuring Storage Plug-in for Containers

:::note
Sample files are available at [github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi](https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi) in `csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/sample`.
:::

## Secret Settings

The Secret contains the storage URL, username, password, and host mode options.

```yaml title="secret-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Secret
metadata:
  name: secret-sample         # (1)
type: Opaque
data:
  url: aHR0cDovLzE3Mi4xNi4xLjE=   # (2) base64-encoded storage URL
  user: VXNlcjAx                    # (3) base64-encoded username
  password: UGFzc3dvcmQwMQ==        # (4) base64-encoded password
  hostModeOptions: ODgsODENCg==     # (5) base64-encoded host mode options
```

| Field | Description                                                                                                                                                   |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | Secret name                                                                                                                                                   |
| (2)   | Base64-encoded storage URL. Use SVP IP for VSP 5000 series; controller IP for VSP E/F/G series; service IP for VSP One B20 series and VSP One Block High End. |
| (3)   | Base64-encoded username: `echo -n "User01" \| base64`                                                                                                         |
| (4)   | Base64-encoded password: `echo -n "Password01" \| base64`                                                                                                     |
| (5)   | Base64-encoded host mode options (e.g., `echo -n "88,81" \| base64`). Default options: 2, 22, 25, 68, 91.                                                     |

:::note
To add custom host mode options, specify them in `hostModeOptions`. You must delete and recreate the Pod to apply them.
:::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/expanding-volumes.md
---

# Expanding Volumes

HSPC supports online volume expansion. The StorageClass must have `allowVolumeExpansion: true` (which is set in the sample StorageClass by default).

## Procedure

### Edit the PVC to request more storage
```bash
kubectl edit pvc pvc-sample
```
Update the storage request:
```yaml
spec:
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi   # [!code ++]
```
### Verify the PVC capacity has been updated
```bash
kubectl get pvc pvc-sample
```
```
NAME         STATUS   VOLUME                                     CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   STORAGECLASS   AGE
pvc-sample   Bound    pvc-cf8c6089-0386-4c39-8037-e1520a986a7d  10Gi       RWO            sc-sample      2d
```
### Verify the filesystem was expanded inside the Pod
```bash
kubectl exec -it pod-sample -- df -h /data
```

:::note
For HRPC replication volumes, expand the **secondary site first**, then the primary. See [Expanding Replication Volumes](/container-plugins/hrpc/v3.17.4/expanding-volumes.md).
:::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/index.md
---

# HSPC Quick Reference Guide




---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/installation.md
---

# Installing Storage Plug-in for Containers

:::warning
If there is a previous version of HSPC installed, **remove it before proceeding** with installation.
:::


**OpenShift**

## Installation on OpenShift
HSPC is deployed on OpenShift using the Operator from OperatorHub.
### Access OperatorHub
Open the OpenShift web console and navigate to OperatorHub.
### Search and install the Operator
Search for **Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers** and install it.
:::note
Select the following settings in Operator Subscription:
- **Installation mode:** Select "A specific namespace on the cluster" and specify any namespace.
- **Update approval:** Select "Manual" and approve the Install Plan.
:::
For disconnected environments, mirror the `certified-operators` catalog before installation. See [OpenShift mirroring documentation](https://docs.openshift.com/container-platform/latest/disconnected/mirroring/installing-mirroring-installation-images.html).
### Confirm the Operator status is Succeeded
Verify the Operator Pod is **Running**.
### Create the HSPC instance
Click **Create Instance** on the Operator Details page. Click **Create**. For advanced configuration, see [Instance Configuration](#instance-configuration) below.
### Verify HSPC is ready
```bash
oc get hspc -n <namespace>
```
```
NAME   READY   AGE
hspc   true    30s
```


**Kubernetes**

## Installation on Kubernetes
### Clone the repository
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/operator
```
### Create the Operator namespace
```bash
kubectl create -f hspc-operator-namespace.yaml
kubectl get namespaces | grep hspc-operator-system
```
```
hspc-operator-system   Active   53s
```
### Deploy the Operator
```bash
kubectl create -f hspc-operator.yaml
kubectl get deployment -n hspc-operator-system
```
```
NAME                                 READY   UP-TO-DATE   AVAILABLE   AGE
hspc-operator-controller-manager     1/1     1            1           14s
```
### Configure and create the HSPC instance
Edit `hspc_v1_hspc.yaml` if needed. For advanced configuration, see [Instance Configuration](#instance-configuration) below.
```bash
kubectl create -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
```
### Verify HSPC is ready
```bash
kubectl get hspc -n <namespace>
```
```
NAME   READY   AGE
hspc   true    30s
```


## Instance Configuration \{#instance-configuration}

Configure HSPC by editing the CustomResource YAML file (`hspc_v1_hspc.yaml`).

| Parameter                                    | Description                                                                                            |
| -------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| `spec.imagePullSecrets`                      | Specify if a Secret is required to pull an image                                                       |
| `spec.controller.containers.name`            | Name of a container in `hspc-csi-controller` to configure (e.g., `hspc-csi-driver`, `csi-provisioner`) |
| `spec.controller.containers.image`           | Image name for `hspc-csi-controller`                                                                   |
| `spec.controller.containers.imagePullPolicy` | Image pull policy for `hspc-csi-controller` (default: `IfNotPresent`)                                  |
| `spec.controller.containers.env`             | Environment variables for `hspc-csi-controller`                                                        |
| `spec.controller.containers.args`            | Entry point arguments for `hspc-csi-controller`                                                        |
| `spec.controller.tolerations`                | Tolerations for the `hspc-csi-controller` Pod                                                          |
| `spec.controller.affinity.nodeAffinity`      | Node affinity for the `hspc-csi-controller` Pod                                                        |
| `spec.node.containers.name`                  | Name of a container in `hspc-csi-node` to configure                                                    |
| `spec.node.containers.image`                 | Image name for `hspc-csi-node`                                                                         |
| `spec.node.containers.env`                   | Environment variables for `hspc-csi-node`                                                              |
| `spec.node.tolerations`                      | Tolerations for the `hspc-csi-node` Pod                                                                |
| `spec.node.affinity.nodeAffinity`            | Node affinity for the `hspc-csi-node` Pod                                                              |

## Environment Variables

| Variable                 | Description                                                                 | Default |
| ------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------- |
| `SPC_VERIFY_CERTIFICATE` | If `true`, verifies the TLS certificate of the storage in HTTPS connections | `false` |
| `TZ`                     | Timezone for logging (e.g., `Asia/Tokyo`)                                   | `UTC`   |

### Example: Enabling Certificate Verification

```yaml title="hspc_v1_hspc.yaml"
apiVersion: csi.hitachi.com/v1
kind: HSPC
metadata:
  name: hspc
  namespace: <namespace>
spec:
  controller:
    containers:
      - name: hspc-csi-driver
        env:
          - name: SPC_VERIFY_CERTIFICATE
            value: "true"
```

After editing, uninstall and reinstall HSPC to apply the changes.

## Multipath Configuration via DaemonSet

Deploy and manage multipath settings on Kubernetes nodes through a DaemonSet:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<version>/sample
kubectl create -f rosa-daemonset.yaml
```

Verify DaemonSet deployment:

```bash
kubectl get daemonset hspc-iscsi-init -n <namespace>
```

On each node, verify `multipathd` is active:

```bash
systemctl status multipathd
cat /etc/multipath.conf
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/overview.md
---

# Overview and Prerequisites

## Architecture

The following diagram shows how Storage Plug-in for Containers integrates a Kubernetes or OpenShift cluster with Hitachi storage via the Container Storage Interface (CSI):

![HSPC architecture — Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster: csi-controller as a Deployment on the control plane (REST API to Hitachi VSP storage), csi-node as a DaemonSet on each worker node (FC / iSCSI / NVMe data path to volumes)](/images/hspc/hspc-architecture.svg)

The `csi-controller` runs in the Control Plane and communicates with the storage system REST API. The `csi-node` pods run on each worker node and provide direct Fibre Channel / iSCSI / NVMe connectivity to storage volumes.

## Supported Container Orchestrators

| Container Orchestrator                  | Supported Versions     | Notes                                                   |
| --------------------------------------- | ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------- |
| Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform    | 4.18, 4.19, 4.20       | —                                                       |
| Kubernetes                              | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 | —                                                       |
| Rancher Kubernetes Engine 2 (RKE2)      | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 | References to "Kubernetes" in this guide apply to RKE2. |
| Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) | 1.30, 1.31, 1.32, 1.33 | References to "Kubernetes" in this guide apply to EKS.  |

## Server Requirements

| Component        | Requirement                                                                      |
| ---------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| CPU              | x86\_64                                                                          |
| Operating system | See the [Release Notes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/release-notes.md)        |
| Interface        | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC, NVMe/TCP (bare metal); iSCSI, NVMe/TCP (VMs) |

## Storage Requirements

### VSP Family and VSP One Block

| Component              | Requirement                                                                                                    |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Model and SVOS version | See [Release Notes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/release-notes.md)                                          |
| Interface              | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC (NVMe/TCP **not** supported)                                                |
| Host group             | HSPC creates host groups using predefined naming rules. Do **not** use HSPC host groups for any other purpose. |
| User account           | Built-in Storage Administrator (View & Modify) user group (or custom group with equivalent roles)              |
| Required licenses      | Dynamic Provisioning (DP), Hitachi Thin Image (HTI), Hitachi Thin Image Advanced (HTIA) for VSP One Block      |
| SVP                    | Single and dual SVP supported. SVP is **not** required for VSP One Block.                                      |

### VSP One SDS Block

| Component    | Requirement                                                                       |
| ------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Version      | See [Release Notes](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/release-notes.md)             |
| Interface    | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe/TCP (NVMe/TCP only for VSP One SDS Block 01.16.00.4x+) |
| User account | Storage role (non-multitenancy); VpsStorage role (multitenancy)                   |

## Network Requirements

HSPC uses **port 80 or 443** for REST API connections. Configure your firewall accordingly.

:::note
HSPC does **not** support IPv6. Use IPv4.
:::

## Pre-installation Tasks

### Server Pre-installation

| Component     | Task                                                                                                    |
| ------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Hypervisor    | Set up the hypervisor if using virtual machines. Tested with VMware vSphere 7.0/8.0.                    |
| Fibre Channel | Verify HBA is installed on nodes with FC connections.                                                   |
| iSCSI         | Verify iSCSI initiator software is installed. HSPC does **not** support IQNs with uppercase characters. |
| NVMe over FC  | Supported on Ubuntu 20.04/22.04/24.04, RHEL 9, and RHEL CoreOS. Install `nvme-cli`.                     |
| NVMe/TCP      | Supported on RHEL 9 and RHEL CoreOS. Install `nvme-cli` and load `nvme-tcp` module if needed.           |
| Multipath     | Use **Device Mapper Multipath** for FC/iSCSI. Use **Native NVMe Multipath** for NVMe over FC/TCP.       |

### Device Mapper Multipath Settings

Enable Device Mapper Multipath with `user_friendly_names yes`:

```text title="/etc/multipath.conf"
defaults {
  user_friendly_names yes
  find_multipaths yes
}
blacklist {
}
```

OS-specific documentation:

- [RHEL 8 DM Multipath](https://access.redhat.com/documentation/en-us/red_hat_enterprise_linux/8/html/configuring_device_mapper_multipath/configuring-dm-multipath_configuring-device-mapper-multipath)
- [RHEL 9 DM Multipath](https://docs.redhat.com/en/documentation/red_hat_enterprise_linux/9/html/configuring_device_mapper_multipath/configuring-dm-multipath_configuring-device-mapper-multipath)
- [Ubuntu Multipathing](https://ubuntu.com/server/docs/device-mapper-multipathing-introduction)

For **OpenShift**, apply multipath settings via MachineConfig:

```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
```

```bash
# Encode custom multipath settings
cat multipath-sample.conf | base64 -w0
```

```yaml title="multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: machineconfiguration.openshift.io/v1
kind: MachineConfig
metadata:
  name: multipath-machineconfig-sample
  labels:
    machineconfiguration.openshift.io/role: worker
spec:
  config:
    ignition:
      version: 3.2.0
    storage:
      files:
        - contents:
            source: data:text/plain;charset=utf-8;base64,<base64-encoded-config>
          filesystem: root
          mode: 400
          path: /etc/multipath.conf
```

```bash
oc apply -f multipath-machineconfig-sample.yaml
```

:::note
MachineConfig applies to compute nodes only. After creation, all compute nodes restart one by one, and `/etc/multipath.conf` is created on all compute nodes.
:::

### Storage Pre-installation for VSP Family and VSP One Block

| Component        | Task                                                                                                                     |
| ---------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| Program products | Enable DP, HTI, and HTIA (VSP One Block) licenses                                                                        |
| Pool             | Create a DP pool. Dynamic Tiering is **not** supported. VSP One Block requires a pool configured with flash media (SSD). |
| FC connection    | Use a Fibre Channel switch. Configure ports: Connection Type=P-to-P, Fabric=ON, Security=Enabled.                        |
| iSCSI connection | Enable port security via Storage Navigator.                                                                              |
| NVMe over FC     | Create an NVM subsystem. Enable namespace security. Set FC port to NVMe mode.                                            |

### Host Group and iSCSI Target Naming Rules

HSPC automatically creates and searches host groups using these naming rules.

**Host group naming rule:**

```
spc-<wwn1>-<wwn2>-<wwn3>
```

- `<wwn1>`, `<wwn2>`, `<wwn3>` are the sorted WWNs of the host.
- If a host has more than 3 WWNs, only the 3 lowest names are used.

**iSCSI target naming rule:**

```
spc-<any-string>
```

- Auto-created as `spc-<hashed-IQN>`.
- Existing targets must be renamed to follow the `spc-` prefix rule.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/provisioning.md
---

# Provisioning Volumes

## PersistentVolumeClaim Settings

```yaml title="pvc-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample        # (1)
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce        # (2)
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi         # (3)
  storageClassName: sc-sample  # (4)
```

| Field | Description                                         |
| ----- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | PVC name                                            |
| (2)   | `ReadWriteOnce`, `ReadWriteMany`, or `ReadOnlyMany` |
| (3)   | Volume size                                         |
| (4)   | StorageClass name                                   |

:::note

- For `ReadWriteMany`, see [Raw Block Volume](#raw-block-volume) below.
- For `ReadOnlyMany`, see [ReadOnlyMany](#readonlymany) below.
- To use an existing storage volume, see [Static Provisioning](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/storage-classes.md#static-provisioning).

:::

## Pod Settings
