```yaml title="pod-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: pod-sample           # (1)
spec:
  containers:
    - name: my-busybox
      image: busybox
      volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: "/data"  # (2)
          name: sample-volume
      command: ["sleep", "1000000"]
      imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
  volumes:
    - name: sample-volume
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: pvc-sample # (3)
```

## Full Workflow — Create and Verify

```bash title="Create all resources"
kubectl create -f secret-sample.yaml
kubectl create -f sc-sample.yaml
kubectl create -f pvc-sample.yaml
kubectl create -f pod-sample.yaml
```

Verify each resource:

```bash
kubectl get secret
kubectl get sc
kubectl get pvc
kubectl get pod
```

Expected output:

```
NAME         STATUS   VOLUME                                     CAPACITY   ACCESS MODES   STORAGECLASS   AGE
pvc-sample   Bound    pvc-cf8c6089-0386-4c39-8037-e1520a986a7d  1Gi        RWO            sc-sample      28s
```

```bash
kubectl describe pv <pv-name>
```

```
VolumeHandle: 01--scsi--900000070010--914--spc-ecdf100f22
VolumeAttributes:
  connectionType=fc
  ldevIDDec=914
  ldevIDHex=03:92
  nickname=spc-ecdf100f22
  ports=CL5-C
  size=1Gi
```

:::warning
Do **not** change the nickname of an LDEV created by HSPC.
:::

## Delete Resources

Delete in reverse order — Pod first, then PVC, then StorageClass, then Secret:

```bash
kubectl delete pod pod-sample
kubectl delete pvc pvc-sample
kubectl delete sc sc-sample
kubectl delete secret secret-sample
```

## PVC Error Handling

- **Creation failure:** A PVC object may be created without a corresponding PV. Delete the PVC:
  ```bash
  kubectl delete pvc <PVC-name>
  ```
- **Deletion failure:** A PVC may be deleted but the PV and storage asset (LDEV) remain. View the volume properties:
  ```bash
  kubectl describe pv <PV-name>
  ```
  Then delete the PV and the LDEV via the storage system's management interface.

## Raw Block Volume

ReadWriteMany access requires raw block volume mode:

```yaml title="pvc-sample-rwx-raw.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-sample-rwx
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteMany
  volumeMode: Block
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
```

```yaml title="pod-sample-rwx.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: pod-sample-rwx
spec:
  containers:
    - name: my-container
      image: ubuntu
      volumeDevices:
        - name: raw-volume
          devicePath: /dev/sdx
  volumes:
    - name: raw-volume
      persistentVolumeClaim:
        claimName: pvc-sample-rwx
```

## ReadOnlyMany

ReadOnlyMany mode can be used by creating a PVC with ReadOnlyMany access mode. The volume is mounted as read-only on all consumer Pods.



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/snapshots.md
---

# Volume Snapshots

HSPC supports Kubernetes-native volume snapshots using the `VolumeSnapshot` API.

For the full snapshot configuration and commands, see [Snapshots in the Storage Classes guide](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/storage-classes.md#snapshots).

## Listing Snapshots

```bash
kubectl get volumesnapshot
```

```
NAME              READYTOUSE   SOURCEPVC    SOURCESNAPSHOTCONTENT   RESTORESIZE   SNAPSHOTCLASS   SNAPSHOTCONTENT
snapshot-sample   true         pvc-sample                           1Gi           snapshotclass-sample      snapcontent-xxxxxxxx
```

## Verifying Snapshot Content

```bash
kubectl describe volumesnapshot snapshot-sample
```

## Restoring from a Snapshot

Create a new PVC with `dataSource` referencing the snapshot. See [Snapshots](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/storage-classes.md#snapshots).

## Deleting a Snapshot

```bash
kubectl delete volumesnapshot snapshot-sample
```



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/storage-classes.md
---

# Storage Classes and Advanced Usage

## StorageClass Settings

The StorageClass file contains storage settings that are necessary for Storage Plug-in for Containers to work with your environment. The following samples provide information about the required parameters.

:::warning
After creating a StorageClass and PVC, re-creating the StorageClass will **not** affect existing PVCs.
:::


**VSP Family / VSP One Block**

```yaml title="sc-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample                               # (1)
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"                         # (2)
  poolID: "1"                                   # (3)
  portID: CL1-A,CL2-A                           # (4)
  connectionType: fc                            # (5)
  storageEfficiency: "CompressionDeduplication" # (6)
  storageEfficiencyMode: "PostProcess"          # (7)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4               # (8)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"         # (9)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"           # (10)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```
| Field | Description                                                                                                                                             |
| ----- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | StorageClass name                                                                                                                                       |
| (2)   | Storage serial number                                                                                                                                   |
| (3)   | HDP pool ID                                                                                                                                             |
| (4)   | Port ID (comma-separated for multipath). Not required for NVMe over FC.                                                                                 |
| (5)   | Connection type: `fc`, `iscsi`, or `nvme-fc`. Default: `fc`. For NVMe over FC, also add `nvmSubsystemID`.                                               |
| (6)   | Adaptive data reduction: `Compression`, `CompressionDeduplication`, or `Disabled`. Default: `Disabled`. VSP One B20 series does not support `Disabled`. |
| (7)   | Execution mode for adaptive data reduction: `Inline` or `PostProcess`. Only when (6) is set.                                                            |
| (8)   | Filesystem type: `ext4` or `xfs`. Default: `ext4`.                                                                                                      |
| (9)   | Secret name                                                                                                                                             |
| (10)  | Secret namespace                                                                                                                                        |


**VSP One SDS Block**

```yaml title="sc-sample-vsp-one-sds-block.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-sample-vsp-one-sds-block    # (1)
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  storageType: vsp-one-sds-block       # (2) Required for VSP One SDS Block
  connectionType: fc                   # (3) fc, iscsi, or nvme-tcp
  storageEfficiency: Disabled          # (4) Compression or Disabled
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4      # (5)
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```
| Field | Description                                              |
| ----- | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| (1)   | StorageClass name                                        |
| (2)   | Storage type for SDS Block. Must be `vsp-one-sds-block`. |
| (3)   | Connection type: `fc`, `iscsi`, or `nvme-tcp`.           |
| (4)   | Adaptive data reduction: `Compression` or `Disabled`.    |
| (5)   | Filesystem type: `ext4` or `xfs`. Default: `ext4`.       |


## Static Provisioning

Use static provisioning to use an existing storage volume as a PersistentVolume.

### Requirements

- The volume must exist on the storage system before creating the PV.
- The `claimRef` must be correctly specified to bind the PV to a PVC.

:::note
If you are mixing dynamic and static provisioning, verify there are no PVs with `STATUS: Available` that have no `claimRef` set before creating new PVCs. Unbound PVs without `claimRef` may be incorrectly associated with dynamically-created PVCs.
:::

### Creating Secret and StorageClass for Static Provisioning

Use the same Secret and StorageClass format as for dynamic provisioning. See [Configuration](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/configuration.md).

### Creating a PV

Specify the LDEV ID and other volume properties in the PV manifest.

```yaml title="pv-static-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolume
metadata:
  name: pv-static-sample
  annotations:
    pv.kubernetes.io/provisioned-by: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
spec:
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  capacity:
    storage: 10Gi
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  persistentVolumeReclaimPolicy: Retain
  volumeMode: Filesystem
  csi:
    driver: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
    volumeHandle: "<serial-number>--<connection-type>--<storage-device-id>--<ldev-id-dec>--<nickname>"
    fsType: ext4
    nodeStageSecretRef:
      name: secret-sample
      namespace: default
    nodePublishSecretRef:
      name: secret-sample
      namespace: default
    controllerPublishSecretRef:
      name: secret-sample
      namespace: default
    volumeAttributes:
      connectionType: fc
      portID: CL1-A,CL2-A
      serialNumber: "54321"
  claimRef:
    name: pvc-static-sample
    namespace: default
```

### Creating a PVC for Static Provisioning

Bind a PVC to the static PV by specifying `volumeName`:

```yaml title="pvc-static-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-static-sample
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 10Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  volumeName: pv-static-sample
```

## Snapshots

Create a VolumeSnapshotClass before using snapshots:

```yaml title="volumesnapshotclass-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshotClass
metadata:
  name: snapshotclass-sample
driver: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
deletionPolicy: Delete
parameters:
  poolID: "1"
  retentionPeriod: "1"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/snapshotter-secret-namespace: "default"
```

:::note
`retentionPeriod` is optional. Specify a value between 1 and 12,288 hours to create immutable snapshots. Immutable snapshots are supported only on VSP One B20 series storage systems.
:::

Create a snapshot of a PVC:

```yaml title="snapshot-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: snapshot.storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: VolumeSnapshot
metadata:
  name: snapshot-sample
spec:
  volumeSnapshotClassName: snapshotclass-sample
  source:
    persistentVolumeClaimName: pvc-sample
```

Restore a PVC from a snapshot:

```yaml title="pvc-from-snapshot.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-from-snapshot
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  dataSource:
    name: snapshot-sample
    kind: VolumeSnapshot
    apiGroup: snapshot.storage.k8s.io
```

## Cloning a PVC

Clone an existing PVC:

```yaml title="pvc-clone-sample.yaml"
apiVersion: v1
kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
metadata:
  name: pvc-clone-sample
spec:
  accessModes:
    - ReadWriteOnce
  resources:
    requests:
      storage: 1Gi
  storageClassName: sc-sample
  dataSource:
    name: pvc-sample
    kind: PersistentVolumeClaim
```

## Resource Partitioning

Resource partitioning enables multi-tenant isolation using Hitachi Virtual Storage Machine (VSM) resource groups. The correct approach is to assign each Kubernetes cluster to exactly one Resource Group.

:::note
If you use resource partitioning, configure it **before** creating any StorageClasses or PVCs.
:::

**Supported — one Resource Group per Kubernetes cluster:**

![Resource partitioning multi-cluster overview — four Kubernetes clusters mapped across two resource groups: production #1 and #2 use Resource Group #1 (blue), test #1 and #2 use Resource Group #2 (pink), accessed via Shared Ports](/images/hspc/resource-partitioning-overview.svg)

Each Kubernetes cluster maps to exactly one Resource Group. Production clusters use Resource Group #1; test clusters use Resource Group #2. Storage ports are shared but LDEV IDs, Host Group IDs, and Pools are isolated per resource group.

**Unsupported (Example 1) — mixing a resource group StorageClass with meta\_resource in the same cluster:**

![Resource partitioning unsupported example 1 — single Kubernetes cluster with both a resource-group-specific StorageClass and a meta\_resource StorageClass](/images/hspc/resource-partitioning-unsupported.svg)

You cannot use both a resource-group-specific Secret/StorageClass and a meta\_resource (no resource group) Secret/StorageClass in the same Kubernetes cluster.

**Unsupported (Example 2) — two Resource Groups from the same storage mapped to one cluster:**

![Resource partitioning unsupported example 2 — single Kubernetes cluster with StorageClasses for both Resource Group #1 and Resource Group #2 from the same storage system](/images/hspc/resource-partitioning-unsupported2.svg)

You cannot map multiple resource groups from the same storage system to the same Kubernetes cluster.

Add the `resourceGroup` parameter to the StorageClass:

```yaml title="sc-resource-partitioned.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-resource-partitioned
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"
  poolID: "1"
  portID: CL1-A,CL2-A
  connectionType: fc
  resourceGroup: "1"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```

:::warning
All replications on a storage system using resource partitioning with HRPC must belong to the same resource group. See [HRPC Appendix](/container-plugins/hrpc/v3.17.4/appendix.md#resource-partitioning) for HRPC-specific requirements.
:::

## Stretched PVC

Stretched PVC provides high-availability by spanning a PVC across two storage systems at two different sites. It uses **GAD (Global Active Device)** — Hitachi's active-active synchronous replication technology — so both storage systems serve I/O simultaneously and data is always consistent across sites.

The following diagram shows the topology of a Stretched Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster across two sites with GAD replication:

![Stretched PVC topology — Site A and Site B nodes in a single stretched Kubernetes cluster, each connected to their own Storage System with GAD (Global Active Device) active-active replication in between](/images/hspc/stretched-pvc-topology.svg)

The following diagram shows the data path connections used by Stretched PVC (straight path and cross path):

![Stretched PVC data paths — straight path from each node to its local storage system and cross path from each node to the remote storage system](/images/hspc/stretched-pvc-paths.svg)

### Requirements

- Two storage systems at two separate sites with the same software version
- **Global Active Device (GAD)** license on both storage systems
- Remote replication paths (inter-site links) configured between both storage systems

### Creating a Stretched PVC

Create a StorageClass with `stretched: "true"` and both storage systems configured:

```yaml title="sc-stretched.yaml"
apiVersion: storage.k8s.io/v1
kind: StorageClass
metadata:
  name: sc-stretched
  annotations:
    kubernetes.io/description: Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers
provisioner: hspc.csi.hitachi.com
reclaimPolicy: Delete
volumeBindingMode: Immediate
allowVolumeExpansion: true
parameters:
  serialNumber: "54321"
  poolID: "1"
  portID: CL1-A
  connectionType: fc
  stretched: "true"
  remoteSerialNumber: "67890"
  remotePoolID: "2"
  remotePortID: CL1-A
  csi.storage.k8s.io/fstype: ext4
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/provisioner-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-publish-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/node-stage-secret-namespace: "default"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-name: "secret-sample"
  csi.storage.k8s.io/controller-expand-secret-namespace: "default"
```

:::note
Stretched PVC metrics collection by HSPP is **not supported**.
:::



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/troubleshooting.md
---

# Troubleshooting

## Collecting Information for Support

### Required Information

| Information                         | How to Collect                                                         |
| ----------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Kubernetes/OpenShift cluster info   | `kubectl cluster-info dump -A > dump.txt`                              |
| HSPC logs — csi-controller          | See [Collecting HSPC Logs](#collecting-logs)                           |
| HSPC logs — csi-node                | See [Collecting HSPC Logs](#collecting-logs)                           |
| HSPC manifests                      | YAML files used to install HSPC                                        |
| Secret, StorageClass, PVC manifests | YAML files for storage resources                                       |
| Application manifests               | YAML files for applications using HSPC volumes                         |
| Storage dump (VSP/VSP One Block)    | SVP normal dump (if using SVP), or system dump via maintenance utility |
| Storage info (VSP One SDS Block)    | System dump via maintenance utility                                    |

**Support contact:** [support.hitachivantara.com](https://support.hitachivantara.com/en_us/contact-us.html)

:::tip
For an optional **automated** log bundle (multiple pods, cluster context, and a browser-based viewer), see [Hitachi CSI Log Bundle](/container-plugins/csi-log-bundle.md). It is community-maintained and not a Hitachi Vantara product.
:::

## Collecting HSPC Logs \{#collecting-logs}

```bash
# csi-controller logs
kubectl logs -n <namespace> \
  deployment/hspc-csi-controller \
  -c hspc-csi-driver

# csi-node logs (all nodes)
kubectl logs -n <namespace> \
  daemonset/hspc-csi-node \
  -c hspc-csi-driver
```

:::tip
Set up cluster-level logging to persist logs: [Kubernetes logging documentation](https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/cluster-administration/logging/)
:::

## Viewing PersistentVolume Properties

To find the LDEV ID and other storage properties of a PV:

```bash
kubectl describe pv <PV-name>
```

Look for `VolumeHandle` and `VolumeAttributes` in the output:

```
VolumeHandle: 01--scsi--900000070010--914--spc-ecdf100f22
VolumeAttributes:
  ldevIDDec=914
  ldevIDHex=03:92
  nickname=spc-ecdf100f22
```

## Notes on Force-Deleting a Pod

Forcibly deleting a Pod (`kubectl delete pod --force --grace-period=0`) while it has an HSPC-managed volume attached can leave the volume in an inconsistent state. Before doing this:

1. Verify the node where the Pod was running is completely down or the volume is no longer attached.
2. After force-deletion, verify the volume is not still attached to the node in the storage system.

## Creating and Deleting PVCs Simultaneously

Do **not** create and delete PVCs at the same time. Creating a PVC while another is being deleted can result in storage system inconsistencies.

## Host Group Settings

HSPC automatically manages host groups. Do **not** manually modify host groups created by HSPC (`spc-*` prefix). If you need custom host group settings, configure them before installing HSPC or rename existing host groups to match the naming rule. See [Host group naming rules](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/overview.md#host-group-and-iscsi-target-naming-rules).

## Timeout Errors When Creating or Deleting Pods

If Pod creation or deletion times out with HSPC-managed volumes, check:

1. The `csi-controller` log for storage API errors.
2. Storage system connectivity (port 80 or 443).
3. Whether the storage system operation timed out and needs cleanup manually.

## Scheduling or Creation Failures

If a Kubernetes pod cannot be scheduled or created:

1. Check node selector and affinity settings.
2. Verify `csi-node` DaemonSet is running on the target node:
   ```bash
   kubectl get daemonset hspc-csi-node -n <namespace>
   ```
3. Check `csi-node` logs for attachment errors.

## Virtual LDEV ID Errors

Virtual LDEV ID errors occur when the LDEV ID on the storage system conflicts with an HSPC-managed LDEV. To resolve:

1. Check the LDEV using Storage Navigator.
2. Ensure no LDEV nickname starting with `spc-` is managed outside of HSPC.

## Advanced Configuration

See [Appendix A: Advanced Configuration](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/appendix.md) for:

- Editing the existing LUN configuration
- Migrating existing ports of a StorageClass
- Modifying bulk mapping mode
- Modifying CPU and memory resources for the CSI Controller deployment



---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/uninstalling.md
---

# Upgrading and Uninstalling HSPC

## Upgrading


**OpenShift**

### Upgrade on OpenShift
Upgrade HSPC on OpenShift via OperatorHub:
1. Navigate to **Installed Operators** in the OpenShift web console.
2. Select **Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers**.
3. Approve the pending upgrade under **Subscription**.


**Kubernetes**

### Upgrade on Kubernetes
### Clone the repository and navigate to the new version
```bash
git clone https://github.com/hitachi-vantara/csi-operator-hitachi
cd csi-operator-hitachi/hspc/<new-version>/operator
```
### Apply the updated Operator manifest
```bash
kubectl apply -f hspc-operator.yaml
```
### Apply the updated HSPC instance manifest
```bash
kubectl apply -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
```
### Verify HSPC is ready
```bash
kubectl get hspc -n <namespace>
```
```
NAME   READY   AGE
hspc   true    30s
```


## Re-creating HSPC

Re-creation is required in some scenarios (e.g., recovering from a misconfiguration).

### Delete all Pods using HSPC volumes
### Delete all PVCs
### Delete Storage Plug-in for Containers
See [Uninstalling HSPC](#uninstalling-hspc) below.
### Re-create HSPC
See [Installing HSPC](/container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/quick-reference/installation.md).

## Uninstalling HSPC \{#uninstalling-hspc}


**OpenShift**

### Uninstall on OpenShift
1. Delete the HSPC instance (CustomResource) from the Operator Details page.
2. Uninstall the Operator from **Installed Operators** in the OpenShift web console.


**Kubernetes**

### Uninstall on Kubernetes
### Delete all Pods and PVCs managed by HSPC
```bash
kubectl delete pod <pod-name>
kubectl delete pvc <pvc-name>
```
### Delete the HSPC instance
```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc_v1_hspc.yaml
```
### Delete the Operator
```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc-operator.yaml
```
### Delete the namespace
```bash
kubectl delete -f hspc-operator-namespace.yaml
```




---
url: /container-plugins/hspc/v3.17.4/release-notes.md
---

# HSPC v3.17.4 Release Notes

**Document:** RN-92ADPTR141-30 (February 2026)

This document provides late-breaking information about Hitachi Storage Plug-in for Containers v3.17.4. It includes information not available when the technical documentation was published, as well as known issues and solutions.

:::note
This is a **minor release** that includes performance improvements and bug fixes.
:::

## About This Release

- **Docs:** [docs.hitachivantara.com](https://docs.hitachivantara.com)
- **Downloads:** [support.hitachivantara.com](https://support.hitachivantara.com)
- **Compatibility:** [compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/hspc](https://compatibility.hitachivantara.com/products/hspc/)

## Supported Software

| Software                                | Supported Version |
| --------------------------------------- | ----------------- |
| Kubernetes                              | 1.30 – 1.33       |
| Red Hat OpenShift Container Platform    | 4.18 – 4.20       |
| Rancher Kubernetes Engine 2 (RKE2)      | 1.30 – 1.33       |
| Amazon Elastic Kubernetes Service (EKS) | 1.30 – 1.33       |

**Supported Operating Systems:**

| Operating System                 | Version                                   |
| -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------- |
| Red Hat Enterprise Linux         | 8.2–8.4, 8.6–8.8, 8.10, 9.0, 9.2–9.4, 9.6 |
| Red Hat Enterprise Linux Core OS | 4.18–4.20                                 |
| Ubuntu                           | 20.04, 22.04, 24.04                       |
| Amazon Linux                     | 2                                         |

:::note
NVMe over Fibre Channel and NVMe/TCP are supported **only** on RHEL 9.x, RHEL Core OS, and Ubuntu.
:::

## Supported Storage Systems

| Storage System                                     | Minimum Firmware                            | Protocol                           |
| -------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------- |
| VSP One Block High End                             | A0-05-20-00/05                              | Fibre Channel                      |
| VSP One B24, B26, B28                              | A3-04-20 or later                           | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC |
| VSP E1090, E1090H                                  | 93-06-01 or later (NVMe over FC: 93-07-21+) | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC |
| VSP 5100, 5500, 5100H, 5500H                       | 90-04-04 or later                           | Fibre Channel, iSCSI               |
| VSP 5200, 5600, 5200H, 5600H                       | 90-08-01 or later (NVMe over FC: 90-09-21+) | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe over FC |
| VSP E590, E790, E990, E590H, E790H                 | 93-02-04 or later                           | Fibre Channel, iSCSI               |
| VSP G350, G370, G700, G900, F350, F370, F700, F900 | 88-06-02 or later                           | Fibre Channel, iSCSI               |
| VSP One SDS Block                                  | 01.08.00.0x or later                        | Fibre Channel, iSCSI, NVMe/TCP     |

## New Features and Enhancements in v3.17.4

- **JSON format support for nvme-cli** — Added JSON format support for both newer and older `nvme-cli` packages to enable detection of NVMe devices.
- **Bulk LUN mapping** — Added support for bulk LUN mapping. Can be disabled by setting the `IS_BULK_MAPPING_MODE_ENABLED` environment variable to `false`.

## Known Limitations

:::warning
Review all limitations before deploying in a production environment.
:::

- **VSP One SDS Block — node addition/removal:** When you add or remove a storage node, Pods created before the change may not function correctly. Restart the plug-in, then restart affected Pods.

- **VSP One SDS Block — NVMe/TCP connections persist:** When removing a storage node, NVMe/TCP connections to the removed node may persist. Restart the node hosting the affected Pod to clear these connections.

- **Stretched PVC primary/secondary swap:** After creating a Stretched PVC using a secret that specifies primary and secondary storage systems, you **cannot** interchange primary and secondary values in that secret.

- **ReadOnlyMany — "read-only file system" error:** Attaching a ReadOnlyMany PVC may result in a "read-only file system" error.
  - **Workaround:** Recreate the Pod with `spec.volumes.persistentVolumeClaim.readOnly: true`.

- **ReadOnlyMany not supported for NVMe over FC.**

- **NVMe over FC — single cluster per NVM subsystem:** Each NVM subsystem must be dedicated to a single Kubernetes cluster. Do **not** share an NVM subsystem across multiple clusters.

- **Multi-AZ not supported** for the cloud model of VSP One SDS Block.

- **OpenShift Virtualization — volume expansion:** After expanding a VM disk volume, the VM may not show the increased size until it is restarted.

- **PVC deletion failure — finalizer:** If PVC deletion fails, manually delete the corresponding PV. If the PV has a finalizer, remove it to allow deletion.

- **ReadOnlyMany is not supported with OpenShift Virtualization.**

- **HTTPS with certificate verification:** The certificate must be installed within the HSPC container image.

- **Stretched PVC pools** can be created only on a virtual storage machine.

- **Mounting a PVC created from a Stretched PVC is not supported.**

## Technology Preview

Technology Preview features provide early access to upcoming innovations for testing only. Not covered by support plans, may not be functionally complete, and not intended for production use.

## Getting Help

- **Support:** [support.hitachivantara.com](https://support.hitachivantara.com/en_us/contact-us.html)
- **Community:** [community.hitachivantara.com](https://community.hitachivantara.com)
- **Doc feedback:** [doc.feedback@hitachivantara.com](mailto:doc.feedback@hitachivantara.com) (include document number RN-92ADPTR141-30 and revision)

url: /reference/index.md

# Storage Model Abbreviations

The following abbreviations are used throughout Hitachi Vantara documentation to refer to specific storage system models. All abbreviations refer to members of the Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform (VSP) product family.

| Abbreviation | Full Name |
| --- | --- |
| VSP One Block | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 24
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 26
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 28
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 85 |
| VSP One Block 20 series | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 20 series- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 24
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 26
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block 28 |
| VSP One Block High End | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform One Block High End |
| VSP E series | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E series- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E590
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E790
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E990
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E1090
- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E590H

- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform E1090H |
| VSP F350 | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform F350 |
| VSP F370 | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform F370 |
| VSP F700 | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform F700 |
| VSP F900 | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform F900 |

| VSP G900 | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform G900 |
| VSP 5000 series | Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform 5000 series- Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform 5100

:::tip
When a document references a storage model abbreviation (for example, "VSP E series"), it applies to all individual models listed under that abbreviation unless otherwise specified.
:::



---
url: /reference/capacity-conventions.md
---

# Capacity Conventions

Hitachi Vantara documentation uses different base conventions for physical and logical storage capacity values. Understanding the distinction is important when sizing storage systems and interpreting product specifications.

## Physical Capacity (Decimal)

Physical storage capacity values (such as disk drive capacity) use SI decimal prefixes:

| Physical Unit   | Value                    |
| --------------- | ------------------------ |
| 1 kilobyte (KB) | 1,000 (10³) bytes        |
| 1 megabyte (MB) | 1,000 KB or 1,000² bytes |
| 1 gigabyte (GB) | 1,000 MB or 1,000³ bytes |
| 1 terabyte (TB) | 1,000 GB or 1,000⁴ bytes |
| 1 petabyte (PB) | 1,000 TB or 1,000⁵ bytes |
| 1 exabyte (EB)  | 1,000 PB or 1,000⁶ bytes |

## Logical Capacity (Binary)

Logical capacity values (such as logical device capacity and cache memory capacity) use binary prefixes:

| Logical Unit                    | Value                    |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------ |
| 1 block                         | 512 bytes                |
| 1 cylinder (Mainframe)          | 870 KB                   |
| 1 cylinder (OPEN-V)             | 960 KB                   |
| 1 cylinder (Other open-systems) | 720 KB                   |
| 1 KB                            | 1,024 (2¹⁰) bytes        |
| 1 MB                            | 1,024 KB or 1,024² bytes |
| 1 GB                            | 1,024 MB or 1,024³ bytes |
| 1 TB                            | 1,024 GB or 1,024⁴ bytes |
| 1 PB                            | 1,024 TB or 1,024⁵ bytes |
| 1 EB                            | 1,024 PB or 1,024⁶ bytes |

:::note
The difference between physical (decimal) and logical (binary) capacities means that the usable logical capacity of a drive will appear smaller than its physical rated capacity. For example, a 1 TB physical drive provides approximately 931 GiB of logical capacity.
:::



---
url: /index.md
---

# Hitachi Vantara

Documentation Portal

> Documentation for Hitachi Vantara storage products — enterprise VSP storage systems, Kubernetes storage solutions, and shared reference material.

## Features

- [💾 **VSP Storage**](/vsp/): Configuration, administration, and user guides for Hitachi Virtual Storage Platform — the enterprise block storage backbone for Hitachi Vantara solutions.
- [🐳 **Storage Plug-ins for Containers**](/container-plugins/): HSPC, HRPC, and HSPP — Kubernetes CSI solutions for Hitachi VSP storage. Enables persistent volumes, cross-cluster replication, and Prometheus monitoring from your container workloads.
- [📖 **Shared Reference**](/reference/): Common reference material — storage model abbreviations and capacity conventions used across all product families.
- 🔍 **Full-Text Search**: Instantly search across all products and versions. Every page, procedure, and parameter is indexed.
- 🏷️ **Versioned Docs**: Access documentation for any product version.
- 🤖 **AI-Friendly**: llms.txt and structured Markdown exports make these docs consumable by AI tools and LLMs.
- ✅ **Always Current**: Git-tracked content with "last updated" timestamps. Every edit is a reviewable diff — no more opaque PDF binaries.
